310	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
311	  % "a ring homomorphism
312	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
313	  %  defined as follows. The images of the variables
314	  %  $x^J_{p,q}$ are given by the entries of the matrix formula
315	  %  $\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J) ^{-1}\,X^I$."
316	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
317	  Let \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\) be the
318	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism out of the chart ring of \(J\) determined,
319	  via the universal property of the polynomial ring, by sending each free
320	  indeterminate \(x^J_{p,q}\) to the \((p,q)\)-entry of the image matrix \(M =
321	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}); equivalently
322	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) as a matrix identity (the
323	  identity \(J\)-block of \(X^J\) matching the identity \(J\)-minor of \(M\)).
324	\end{definition}
325	
326	\begin{lemma}
327	
328	\leanok
329	[The pre-hom sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit]
330	  \label{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}
331	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
332	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
333	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
334	  % affine patches" (read from
335	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
336	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
337	  % "In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
338	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
339	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
340	  The pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre})
341	  sends \(P^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}, the \(I\)-minor determinant of
342	  \(X^J\)) to a unit of \(R^I_J\). Indeed
343	  \[
344	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr)
345	      = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1} = 1/P^I_J,
346	  \]
347	  which is a unit.
348	\end{lemma}
349	\begin{proof}
350	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_minorDet_unit}
351	  \leanok
352	  By \cref{def:gr_transition_pre}, \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(X^J) = M = (X^I_J)^{-1}
353	  X^I\), so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries the \(I\)-minor \(X^J_I\) of \(X^J\) to
354	  the \(I\)-minor of \(M\), which is \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) since
355	  the \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity. Hence \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) =
356	  \det((X^I_J)^{-1}) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\). As \(\det(X^I_J)\) is a unit
357	  (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), so is its inverse; the matrix-inverse
358	  determinant identity uses \cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}.
359	\end{proof}
360	
361	\begin{definition}
362	
363	\leanok
364	[Transition map \(\theta_{I,J}\)]
365	  \label{def:gr_transition}
366	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap}
367	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:mathlib_away_lift, def:gr_affine_chart}
368	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
369	  % affine patches" (read from
370	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L822-L836).
371	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
372	  % "For any $I$ and $J$, a ring homomorphism
373	  %  $\theta_{I,J} : \Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \to \Z[X^I, 1/P^I_J]$ is
374	  %  defined as follows. ...
375	  %  In particular, we have $\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1/P^I_J$,
376	  %  so the map extends to $\Z[X^J, 1/P^J_I]$."
377	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
378	  Because the pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to
379	  R^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) sends \(P^J_I\) to a unit of \(R^I_J\)
380	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}), the universal property of the
381	  away-localisation (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) provides a unique ring
382	  homomorphism extending it along \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I]\).
383	  This extension is the \emph{transition map}
384	  \[
385	    \theta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^J, 1/P^J_I] \longrightarrow
386	    \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J], \qquad \theta_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I .
387	  \]
388	  Dually \(\theta_{I,J}\) is the comorphism of a morphism of schemes
389	  \(U^I_J \to U^J_I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), which we also denote
390	  \(\theta_{I,J}\); it sends the universal matrix \(X^I\) to \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\).
391	\end{definition}
392	
393	\begin{lemma}
394	
395	\leanok
396	[\(\theta_{I,I}\) is the identity]
397	  \label{lem:gr_transition_self}
398	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionMap_self}
399	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
400	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
401	  % affine patches" (read from
402	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L838-L848).
403	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
404	  % "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$".
405	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
406	  For every \(I\), \(\theta_{I,I} = \id_{U^I}\): since \(P^I_I = 1\)
407	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) the minor \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\) is its own
408	  inverse, so \(\theta_{I,I}(X^I) = (X^I_I)^{-1} X^I = X^I\), i.e.\
409	  \(\theta_{I,I}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity ring homomorphism
410	  of \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_I] = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\).
411	\end{lemma}
412	\begin{proof}
413	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_minor_det}
414	  \leanok
415	  When \(J = I\) one has \(P^I_I = \det(1_{d\times d}) = 1\)
416	  (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}), so \(R^I_I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\), the minor \(X^I_I\)
417	  is the identity, and its Cramer inverse is again the identity. Hence the image
418	  matrix is \(M = 1 \cdot X^I = X^I\), the pre-hom sends each \(x^I_{p,q}\) to
419	  itself, and its localisation lift (\cref{def:gr_transition}) is the identity.
420	\end{proof}
421	
422	\subsection*{Project-local matrix and minor bookkeeping}
423	
424	The cocycle computation rests on a handful of elementary matrix-algebra
425	identities over the (localised) chart rings. They are project-internal helpers
426	with no external source; each is verified directly in Lean.
427	
428	\begin{lemma}
429	
430	\leanok
431	[Column submatrix of a product]
432	  \label{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}
433	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.mul_submatrix_col}
434	  Let \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(R)\) and
435	  \(B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times r}(R)\) be matrices over a commutative ring,
436	  and \(g : \{1,\dots,d\} \to \{1,\dots,r\}\) a column-index map. Then taking the
437	  column submatrix of the product equals multiplying by the column submatrix of
438	  the right factor:
439	  \[
440	    (A B)_{\bullet\, g} \;=\; A \,\bigl(B_{\bullet\, g}\bigr).
441	  \]
442	\end{lemma}
443	\begin{proof}
444	
445	\leanok
446	  Entrywise from the definition of matrix multiplication (reindexing the rows by
447	  the identity leaves the contraction index untouched). Proved directly in Lean.
448	\end{proof}
449	
450	\begin{lemma}
451	
452	\leanok
453	[A ring map commutes with the Cramer inverse]
454	  \label{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}
455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.map_nonsing_inv}
456	  \uses{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
457	  Let \(f : R \to S\) be a homomorphism of commutative rings and
458	  \(A \in \operatorname{Mat}_{n\times n}(R)\) a square matrix with \(\det A\) a
459	  unit. Writing \(f_\ast\) for the entrywise application of \(f\), the
460	  nonsingular inverse is natural:
461	  \[
462	    \bigl(f_\ast A\bigr)^{-1} \;=\; f_\ast\bigl(A^{-1}\bigr).
463	  \]
464	\end{lemma}
465	\begin{proof}
466	
467	\leanok
468	  Since \(f_\ast A \cdot f_\ast(A^{-1}) = f_\ast(A A^{-1}) = f_\ast(1_{n\times n})
469	  = 1_{n\times n}\) (using \(A A^{-1} = 1\), \cref{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}),
470	  the matrix \(f_\ast(A^{-1})\) is a right inverse of \(f_\ast A\), hence equals
471	  \((f_\ast A)^{-1}\). Proved directly in Lean.
472	\end{proof}
473	
474	\begin{lemma}
475	
476	\leanok
477	[Functoriality of entrywise application]
478	  \label{lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp}
479	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.map_map_eq_of_comp}
480	  Let \(f : R \to A\), \(g : A \to D\) and \(h : R \to D\) be ring
481	  homomorphisms with \(g \circ f = h\). Then for any matrix \(M\) over \(R\),
482	  applying \(f\) then \(g\) entrywise equals applying \(h\) entrywise:
483	  \(g_\ast(f_\ast M) = h_\ast M\).
484	\end{lemma}
485	\begin{proof}
486	
487	\leanok
488	  Immediate from functoriality of \(\operatorname{Mat}(-)\) (composition of
489	  entrywise maps). Proved directly in Lean.
490	\end{proof}
491	
492	\begin{lemma}
493	
494	\leanok
495	[Inverse cancellation in a composite]
496	  \label{lem:gr_inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}
497	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel}
498	  \uses{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}
499	  Let \(A, B \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times d}(R)\) and
500	  \(M \in \operatorname{Mat}_{d\times e}(R)\) over a commutative ring, with
501	  \(\det A\) a unit. Then
502	  \[
503	    \bigl(B^{-1} A\bigr)\,\bigl(A^{-1} M\bigr) \;=\; B^{-1} M.
504	  \]
505	\end{lemma}
506	\begin{proof}
507	
508	\leanok
509	  By associativity and \(A A^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\)
510	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_mul_nonsing_inv}). Proved directly in Lean.
511	\end{proof}
512	
513	\begin{lemma}
514	
515	\leanok
516	[The \(I\)-minor of \(X^I\) is the identity]
517	  \label{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
519	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix}
520	  The \(I\)-minor of the universal matrix \(X^I\)
521	  (\cref{def:gr_universal_matrix}), with its \(I\)-columns read through the order
522	  isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \xrightarrow{\sim} I\), is the \(d\times d\)
523	  identity: \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\). (This is the normalisation \(P^I_I = 1\)
524	  defining the chart.)
525	\end{lemma}
526	\begin{proof}
527	
528	\leanok
529	  By \cref{def:gr_universal_matrix} the \(q\)-column with \(q \in I\) is the
530	  standard basis vector \(e_p\) where \(p\) is the preimage of \(q\) under the
531	  order isomorphism \(\{1,\dots,d\} \xrightarrow{\sim} I\); so the \(I\)-minor
532	  is the identity. Proved directly in Lean.
533	\end{proof}
534	
535	\begin{lemma}
536	
537	\leanok
538	[The \(J\)-minor of the image matrix is the identity]
539	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
541	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}
542	  The \(J\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
543	  (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}) is the identity:
544	  \[
545	    M_J \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_J \;=\; 1_{d\times d}.
546	  \]
547	\end{lemma}
548	\begin{proof}
549	
550	\leanok
551	  The \(J\)-column submatrix of \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) is
552	  \((X^I_J)^{-1}\) times the \(J\)-column submatrix of \(X^I\), i.e.
553	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_J\), by \cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}; this is
554	  \(1_{d\times d}\) by the left-inverse identity
555	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}). Proved directly in Lean.
556	\end{proof}
557	
558	\begin{lemma}
559	
560	\leanok
561	[The \(I\)-minor of the image matrix is the Cramer inverse]
562	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_I}
563	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_submatrix_I}
564	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}
565	  The \(I\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\)
566	  (\cref{def:gr_image_matrix}) is the Cramer inverse:
567	  \[
568	    M_I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}\, X^I_I \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1}.
569	  \]
570	\end{lemma}
571	\begin{proof}
572	
573	\leanok
574	  By \cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col} the \(I\)-column submatrix of \(M\) is
575	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\), and \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\)
576	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_submatrix_self}). Proved directly in Lean.
577	\end{proof}
578	
579	\begin{lemma}
580	
581	\leanok
582	[\(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) realises the matrix formula]
583	  \label{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
584	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
585	  \uses{def:gr_universal_matrix, def:gr_image_matrix, def:gr_transition_pre, lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}
586	  Applying the pre-localisation hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)
587	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) entrywise to the universal matrix \(X^J\) yields
588	  the image matrix:
589	  \[
590	    (\tilde\theta_{I,J})_\ast\, X^J \;=\; M \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I .
591	  \]
592	\end{lemma}
593	\begin{proof}
594	
595	\leanok
596	  On a free entry \(x^J_{p,q}\) (\(q \notin J\)) this holds by the definition of
597	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) as evaluation at the entries of \(M\)
598	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}). On a column \(q \in J\) the entry of \(X^J\) is
599	  an identity-block entry, which \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) carries to the
600	  corresponding entry of \(M_J = 1_{d\times d}\)
601	  (\cref{lem:gr_imageMatrix_submatrix_self}). Proved directly in Lean.
602	\end{proof}
603	
604	\begin{lemma}
605	
606	\leanok
607	[Image of a cross minor under \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)]
608	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
609	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet}
610	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}
611	  For a third size-\(d\) subset \(K\), the pre-hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) sends the
612	  minor determinant \(P^J_K\) (\cref{def:gr_minor_det}) to the determinant of the
613	  \(K\)-minor of the image matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\):
614	  \[
615	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_K\bigr) \;=\; \det\bigl(M_K\bigr).
616	  \]
617	\end{lemma}
618	\begin{proof}
619	
620	\leanok
621	  The hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) commutes with determinants and with passing to
622	  a column submatrix, so \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_K) =
623	  \det\bigl((\tilde\theta_{I,J})_\ast X^J\bigr)_K\); apply
624	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap}. Proved directly in Lean.
625	\end{proof}
626	
627	\begin{lemma}
628	
629	\leanok
630	[Naturality of the image matrix]
631	  \label{lem:gr_imageMatrix_map_eq}
632	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.imageMatrix_map_eq}
633	  \uses{def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp, def:gr_universalMinorInv, lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv, lem:gr_minorDet_unit, def:gr_universal_minor}
634	  Let \(X\) be a size-\(d\) subset and \(\iota : R^I_X \to D\) a ring
635	  homomorphism lying over the structure map \(R^I \to D\), i.e.\
636	  \(\iota \circ (R^I \to R^I_X) = (R^I \to D)\). Writing \(Y\) for the universal
637	  matrix \(X^I\) base-changed to \(D\) and \(Y_X\) for its \(X\)-minor, one has
638	  \[
639	    \iota_\ast\bigl((X^I_X)^{-1} X^I\bigr) \;=\; (Y_X)^{-1}\, Y .
640	  \]
641	\end{lemma}
642	\begin{proof}
643	
644	\leanok
645	  Distribute \(\iota_\ast\) across the product \(M = (X^I_X)^{-1} X^I\); the
646	  factor \((X^I_X)^{-1}\) passes through \(\iota_\ast\) as a Cramer inverse
647	  (\cref{lem:gr_map_nonsing_inv}, using \cref{lem:gr_minorDet_unit}), and both
648	  factors collapse to base changes over \(D\) by functoriality
649	  (\cref{lem:gr_map_map_eq_of_comp}) since \(\iota\) lies over \(R^I \to D\).
650	  Proved directly in Lean.
651	\end{proof}
652	
653	\subsection*{Triple-overlap rings and the localised transition maps}
654	
655	The cocycle condition \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\) cannot
656	be stated as a naive composition of the landed transition maps of
657	\cref{def:gr_transition}: the codomain of \(\theta_{J,K}\) (the ring
658	\(R^J[1/P^J_K]\)) differs from the domain of \(\theta_{I,J}\) (the ring
659	\(R^J[1/P^J_I]\)). The identity therefore lives over the \emph{triple-overlap}
660	rings obtained by inverting both relevant minors in each chart ring:
661	\[
662	  S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)], \quad
663	  S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)], \quad
664	  S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)].
665	\]
666	
667	\begin{definition}
668	
669	\leanok
670	[Inclusion of an away-localisation into a double one (left)]
671	  \label{def:gr_away_incl_left}
672	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclLeft}
673	  \uses{lem:mathlib_away_lift, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
674	  For \(x, y \in R\), the canonical inclusion
675	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y} : R[1/x] \to R[1/(xy)]\) inverting the extra factor
676	  \(y\), defined as the away-localisation lift (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) of
677	  the structure map \(R \to R[1/(xy)]\) along \(x\) — well-defined because \(x\)
678	  is a unit of \(R[1/(xy)]\) (a factor of the unit \(xy\),
679	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}).
680	\end{definition}
681	
682	\begin{definition}
683	
684	\leanok
685	[Inclusion of an away-localisation into a double one (right)]
686	  \label{def:gr_away_incl_right}
687	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclRight}
688	  \uses{lem:mathlib_away_lift, lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}
689	  For \(x, y \in R\), the canonical inclusion
690	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y} : R[1/y] \to R[1/(xy)]\) inverting the extra factor
691	  \(x\), defined as the away-localisation lift (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) of
692	  \(R \to R[1/(xy)]\) along \(y\) — well-defined since \(y\) is a unit of
693	  \(R[1/(xy)]\) (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_algebraMap_isUnit}).
694	\end{definition}
695	
696	\begin{lemma}
697	
698	\leanok
699	[Left inclusion lies over \(R\)]
700	  \label{lem:gr_awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap}
701	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap}
702	  \uses{def:gr_away_incl_left}
703	  The inclusion \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left})
704	  intertwines the two structure maps:
705	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y} \circ (R \to R[1/x]) = (R \to R[1/(xy)])\).
706	\end{lemma}
707	\begin{proof}
708	
709	\leanok
710	  By the defining property of the away-localisation lift
711	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}). Proved directly in Lean.
712	\end{proof}
713	
714	\begin{lemma}
715	
716	\leanok
717	[Right inclusion lies over \(R\)]
718	  \label{lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap}
719	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap}
720	  \uses{def:gr_away_incl_right}
721	  The inclusion \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right})
722	  intertwines the two structure maps:
723	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y} \circ (R \to R[1/y]) = (R \to R[1/(xy)])\).
724	\end{lemma}
725	\begin{proof}
726	
727	\leanok
728	  By the defining property of the away-localisation lift
729	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}). Proved directly in Lean.
730	\end{proof}
731	
732	\begin{lemma}
733

887	\leanok
888	[Cocycle condition for the transition maps]
889	  \label{lem:gr_cocycle}
890	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.cocycleCondition}
891	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_transition_self, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_cocycle_theta_jk, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ik, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}
892	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
893	  % affine patches" (read from
894	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L838-L848).
895	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
896	  % "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$, and
897	  %  we leave it to the reader to verify that
898	  %  for any three subsets $I$, $J$ and $K$ of $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of
899	  %  cardinality $d$, the co-cycle condition
900	  %  $\theta_{I,K}= \theta_{I,J}\theta_{J,K}$
901	  %  is satisfied."
902	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
903	  For any three subsets \(I, J, K \subseteq \{1, \dots, r\}\) of cardinality
904	  \(d\), the transition maps of \cref{def:gr_transition} satisfy the cocycle
905	  condition. As morphisms of schemes, on the triple overlap inside \(U^I\),
906	  \[
907	    \theta_{I,K} \;=\; \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J},
908	  \]
909	  equivalently, as ring homomorphisms,
910	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{I,J} \circ \theta_{J,K}\) (the two read the same
911	  identity through the order-reversal of \(\Spec\)). Together with
912	  \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\), this is exactly the compatibility datum required to
913	  glue the charts.
914	\end{lemma}
915	
916	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Note that $\theta_{I,I}$ is identity on $U^I_I = U^I$, and
917	% we leave it to the reader to verify that
918	% for any three subsets $I$, $J$ and $K$ of $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of
919	% cardinality $d$, the co-cycle condition
920	% $\theta_{I,K}= \theta_{I,J}\theta_{J,K}$
921	% is satisfied."  (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex,
922	% L838-L848.)
923	\begin{proof}
924	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_image_matrix, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities, lem:gr_transition_self, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_cocycle_theta_jk, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ik, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq}
925	  \leanok
926	  We verify the scheme-morphism form
927	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) by a direct matrix
928	  computation, valid over the open locus of \(U^I\) on which all the minors
929	  involved are invertible (namely \(P^I_J\) and \(P^I_K\), which suffices since
930	  the maps are determined by their restriction to a dense open and all rings are
931	  domains; the computation is an identity of matrices with entries in
932	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J, 1/P^I_K]\)).
933	
934	  Start from the matrix \(X^I\) (the universal point of \(U^I\)). By
935	  \cref{def:gr_transition},
936	  \[
937	    M^J \;:=\; \theta_{I,J}(X^I) \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
938	  \]
939	  a \(d \times r\) matrix whose \(J\)-minor is the identity, hence a point of
940	  \(U^J\). Its \(K\)-minor is
941	  \[
942	    M^J_K \;=\; (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_K,
943	    \qquad\text{so}\qquad
944	    (M^J_K)^{-1} \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J,
945	  \]
946	  using \((AB)^{-1} = B^{-1} A^{-1}\) for the invertible \(d \times d\) matrices
947	  \(X^I_J\) and \(X^I_K\). Applying \(\theta_{J,K}\) to \(M^J\) gives
948	  \[
949	    \theta_{J,K}(M^J) \;=\; (M^J_K)^{-1} M^J
950	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I_J \,\cdot\, (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I
951	    \;=\; (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I,
952	  \]
953	  the middle product \(X^I_J (X^I_J)^{-1} = 1_{d\times d}\) cancelling by
954	  associativity of matrix multiplication. The right-hand side is precisely
955	  \(\theta_{I,K}(X^I) = (X^I_K)^{-1} X^I\). Thus
956	  \(\theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,K}\) agree on the
957	  universal matrix \(X^I\), hence agree as morphisms \(U^I \supseteq U^I_J \cap
958	  U^I_K \to U^K\). The special case \(K = I\) (resp.\ \(J = I\)) together with
959	  \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\) shows each \(\theta_{I,J}\) is an isomorphism onto its
960	  image with inverse \(\theta_{J,I}\), since
961	  \(\theta_{J,I} \circ \theta_{I,J} = \theta_{I,I} = \id\).
962	\end{proof}

964	\section{Scheme-level glue-data layer}
965	\label{sec:gr_glue_layer}
966	
967	The transition data of \cref{sec:gr_transition} lives at the level of rings. To
968	feed it to a scheme-gluing machine we must re-express each piece as a morphism in
969	the category of schemes: the charts \(U^I\), the principal-open overlaps
970	\(U^I_J\), their inclusions, and the transitions \(\theta_{I,J}\) all become
971	schemes and scheme morphisms by applying the contravariant functor \(\Spec\).
972	The lemmas of this section record those translations together with the two
973	genuinely geometric facts needed to assemble a glue datum: that each inclusion
974	\(U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\) is an open immersion, and that the fibre product of
975	two principal opens of \(\Spec A\) is again a principal open. They are
976	project-internal bridge results with no external source beyond the gluing
977	construction of \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}.
978

819	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the Grassmannian glue data
820	
821	We assemble the affine charts, their principal-open overlaps, the transition
822	isomorphisms and the cocycle into `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData`, indexed by
823	the size-`d` subsets `{I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}`. Its `.glued` is the
824	Grassmannian scheme `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ`.
825	
826	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_glued_scheme`
827	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
828	
829	/-- The triple-overlap `t'`-field of the Grassmannian glue data
830	(`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the morphism
831	`U^I_J ×_{U^I} U^I_K ⟶ U^J_K ×_{U^J} U^J_I` reconciling the two pullbacks via the
832	away-pullback identification, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`, and the
833	order-swap isomorphism. Project-local. -/
834	noncomputable def chartTransition' (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
835	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
836	    Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r I J hI hJ) (chartIncl d r I K hI hK) ⟶
837	      Limits.pullback (chartIncl d r J K hJ hK) (chartIncl d r J I hJ hI) :=
838	  (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).hom ≫
839	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK)) ≫
840	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
841	      (awayMulCommEquiv (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).toRingHom) ≫
842	    (awayPullbackIso (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)).inv
843	
844	/-- `awayMulCommEquiv` lies over the base ring: it intertwines the two structure maps
845	`R → R[1/(xy)]` and `R → R[1/(yx)]`. Project-local helper for the `t_fac`/cocycle
846	ring computations. -/
847	lemma awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap {A : Type*} [CommRing A] (x y : A) :
848	    (awayMulCommEquiv x y).toRingHom.comp (algebraMap A (Localization.Away (x * y)))
849	      = algebraMap A (Localization.Away (y * x)) := by
850	  haveI : IsLocalization.Away (y * x) (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
851	    rw [mul_comm y x]; infer_instance
852	  ext a
853	  exact (IsLocalization.algEquiv (Submonoid.powers (y * x))
854	    (Localization.Away (x * y)) (Localization.Away (y * x))).commutes a
855	
856	/-- The ring-hom identity underlying the `t_fac` coherence field of the Grassmannian
857	glue data: over the triple-overlap rings, the localised transition `Θ_{I,J}`
858	pre-composed with the order-swap and right inclusion equals the left inclusion

1138	charts are `affineChart`, overlaps `chartOverlap`, inclusions `chartIncl`, transitions
1139	`chartTransition`, with cocycle `chartTransition'_cocycle`. Indexed by the size-`d` subsets
1140	of `Fin r`. Project-local. -/
1141	noncomputable def theGlueData (d r : ℕ) : Scheme.GlueData where
1142	  J := {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d}
1143	  U I := affineChart d r I.1
1144	  V p := chartOverlap d r p.1.1 p.2.1 p.1.2 p.2.2
1145	  f I J := chartIncl d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1146	  f_id I := chartIncl_self_isIso d r I.1 I.2
1147	  f_open I J := chartIncl_isOpenImmersion d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1148	  t I J := chartTransition d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2
1149	  t_id I := chartTransition_self d r I.1 I.2
1150	  t' I J K := chartTransition' d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1151	  t_fac I J K := chartTransition'_fac d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1152	  cocycle I J K := chartTransition'_cocycle d r I.1 J.1 K.1 I.2 J.2 K.2
1153	
1154	/-- The **Grassmannian scheme** `Gr(d,r)` over `ℤ` (`def:gr_glued_scheme`): the scheme glued
1155	from the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts `U^I` along the Plücker transition isomorphisms.
1156	Project-local. -/
1157	noncomputable def scheme (d r : ℕ) : Scheme :=
1158	  (theGlueData d r).glued
1159	
1160	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the restricted diagonal and separatedness
1161	
1162	Following Nitsure §1, "Separatedness": the Grassmannian is separated over `ℤ` because on
1163	each affine patch `U^I ×_ℤ U^J` of `Gr(d,r) ×_ℤ Gr(d,r)` the restricted diagonal is a
1164	closed immersion, equivalently the comorphism ring map
1165	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J = ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`,
1166	`X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I`, `1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (the comorphism `θ_{I,J}`), is surjective.
1167	We build `δ_{I,J}` as the tensor-product lift of the structure map `R^I → R^I_J` and the
1168	pre-localisation transition hom `θ̃_{I,J}`, and prove it surjective: its image contains
1169	`X^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (the `I`-minor determinant of
1170	`(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`), hence all of `R^I_J`.
1171	
1172	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_separated`
1173	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
1174	
1175	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to the multiplicative inverse of (the image of) `P^I_J`:
1176	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) · P^I_J = 1` in `R^I_J`. This refines `isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`
1177	to the explicit two-sided inverse, used to realise `1/P^I_J` in the image of the diagonal
1178	ring map. Project-local. -/
1179	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1180	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
1181	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1182	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1183	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 := by
1184	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
1185	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
1186	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
1187	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
1188	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
1189	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
1190	  have hu : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
1191	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1192	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1193	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1194	  rw [← hu, ← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
1195	
1196	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map** `δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J`
1197	(`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism of the restricted diagonal
1198	`Δ^{-1}(U^I ×_ℤ U^J) = U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J`. It is the tensor-product lift of the
1199	structure map `R^I → R^I_J` (first factor) and the pre-localisation transition hom
1200	`θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (second factor), so `X^I ⊗ 1 ↦ X^I` and
1201	`1 ⊗ X^J ↦ (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
1202	noncomputable def diagonalRingMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :=
1204	  Algebra.TensorProduct.lift
1205	    (IsScalarTower.toAlgHom ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1206	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))
1207	    (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
1208	    (fun _ _ => Commute.all _ _)
1209	
1210	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the left factor is the structure map: `δ_{I,J}(a ⊗ 1) = a` in `R^I_J`.
1211	Project-local. -/
1212	theorem diagonalRingMap_left (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1213	    (hJ : J.card = d) (a : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :
1214	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] 1)
1215	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a := by
1216	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, mul_one]
1217	  rfl
1218	
1219	/-- `δ_{I,J}` on the right factor is the pre-localisation transition hom:
1220	`δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ b) = θ̃_{I,J}(b)`. Project-local. -/
1221	theorem diagonalRingMap_right (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1222	    (hJ : J.card = d) (b : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ) :
1223	    diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ (1 ⊗ₜ[ℤ] b) = transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ b := by
1224	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_one, one_mul]
1225	
1226	/-- The **restricted-diagonal ring map is surjective** (`lem:gr_separated`): the comorphism
1227	`δ_{I,J} : ℤ[X^I] ⊗_ℤ ℤ[X^J] → R^I_J` of the restricted diagonal is surjective, so the
1228	restricted diagonal `U^I_J → U^I ×_ℤ U^J` is a closed immersion. The image contains the
1229	structure-map image of `R^I` (left factor) and `1/P^I_J = δ_{I,J}(1 ⊗ P^J_I)` (right
1230	factor), which together generate `R^I_J = R^I[1/P^I_J]`. Project-local. -/
1231	theorem diagonalRingMap_surjective (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1232	    (hJ : J.card = d) : Function.Surjective (diagonalRingMap d r I J hI hJ) := by
1233	  intro z
1234	  -- `z · uⁿ = algebraMap a` for some `a` and `n = ` power of `P^I_J`, by the localisation
1235	  -- property, where `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1236	  obtain ⟨⟨a, s⟩, hs⟩ := IsLocalization.surj (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) z
1237	  obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := s.2
1238	  -- The witness pushes the power into the second tensor factor: `a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ`, avoiding
1239	  -- any arithmetic in the tensor-product ring.
1240	  refine ⟨a ⊗ₜ[ℤ] (minorDet d r J I hJ hI ^ n), ?_⟩
1241	  -- `v := θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is the inverse of `u := P^I_J` in `R^I_J`.
1242	  have hvu : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) *
1243	      algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1244	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) = 1 :=
1245	    transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul d r I J hI hJ
1246	  -- `algebraMap ↑s = uⁿ`.
1247	  have hsu : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1248	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (s : _)
1249	      = (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1250	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1251	    rw [← hn, map_pow]
1252	  -- `algebraMap a = z · uⁿ`.
1253	  have key : algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1254	      (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) a
1255	      = z * (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1256	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) ^ n := by
1257	    rw [← hs, hsu]
1258	  -- `δ(a ⊗ (P^J_I)ⁿ) = algebraMap a · vⁿ = z · uⁿ · vⁿ = z · (u·v)ⁿ = z`.
1259	  rw [diagonalRingMap, Algebra.TensorProduct.lift_tmul, map_pow,
1260	    IsScalarTower.coe_toAlgHom', key, mul_assoc, ← mul_pow, mul_comm _
1261	      (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)), hvu, one_pow, mul_one]
1262	
1263	/-- The intersection pullback `U^I ∩ U^J = pullback (ι i) (ι j)` is the chart overlap
1264	`U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`lem:gr_separated`, source identification): from the glue-data
1265	universal property `theGlueData.vPullbackConeIsLimit`, whose cone point is `V (i,j) = chartOverlap`.
1266	This is the `e₂` source iso of the restricted-diagonal closed-immersion argument. Project-local. -/
1267	noncomputable def pullbackιIso (d r : ℕ) (i j : (theGlueData d r).J) :
1268	    Limits.pullback ((theGlueData d r).ι i) ((theGlueData d r).ι j) ≅
1269	      chartOverlap d r i.1 j.1 i.2 j.2 :=
1270	  (Limits.limit.isLimit _).conePointUniqueUpToIso
1271	    ((theGlueData d r).vPullbackConeIsLimit i j)

1073	  \label{def:gr_chart_incl}

1080	  \]

1082	\end{definition}
1083	
1084	\begin{lemma}
1085	
1086	\leanok

1093	  \(U^I\).
1094	\end{lemma}
1095	\begin{proof}
1096	  \uses{def:gr_chart_incl, lem:mathlib_specMap_localizationAway_isOpenImmersion}
1097	  \leanok

1099	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_specMap_localizationAway_isOpenImmersion}) applied to
1100	  \(f = P^I_J\). Proved directly in Lean.
1101	\end{proof}
1102	
1103	\begin{lemma}
1104	
1105	\leanok
1106	[The self-overlap inclusion is an isomorphism]

1108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartIncl_self_isIso}

1112	\end{lemma}

1121	
1122	\begin{definition}
1123	
1124	\leanok
1125	[The scheme-level transition \(U^I_J \to U^J_I\)]
1126	  \label{def:gr_chart_transition}

1128	  \uses{def:gr_transition, def:gr_chart_overlap}

1130	  \(\theta_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] \to R^I[1/P^I_J]\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}):
1131	  \[
1132	    U^I_J \;=\; \Spec R^I[1/P^I_J] \longrightarrow \Spec R^J[1/P^J_I] \;=\; U^J_I .
1133	  \]

1156	\subsection*{The away-localisation pullback isomorphism}
1157	
1158	\begin{definition}
1159	
1160	\leanok
1161	[Pullback of two principal opens of \(\Spec A\)]
1162	  \label{def:gr_away_pullback_iso}
1163	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayPullbackIso}
1164	  \uses{lem:mathlib_pullbackSpecIso, lem:mathlib_isLocalization_away_mul, lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}
1165	  Let \(A\) be a commutative ring and \(x, y \in A\). The fibre product of the two
1166	  basic-open inclusions \(\Spec A[1/x] \to \Spec A \leftarrow \Spec A[1/y]\) is
1167	  canonically isomorphic to the spectrum of the doubly-inverted localisation:
1168	  \[
1169	    \Spec A[1/x] \times_{\Spec A} \Spec A[1/y]
1170	      \;\cong\; \Spec A[1/(xy)].
1171	  \]
1172	  The isomorphism is the composite of the tensor-product identification of the
1173	  pullback (\cref{lem:mathlib_pullbackSpecIso}) with the \(\Spec\) of the
1174	  localisation isomorphism
1175	  \(A[1/x] \otimes_A A[1/y] \cong A[1/(xy)]\)
1176	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_isLocalization_away_mul},
1177	  \cref{lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}). It is stated over a general base
1178	  ring \(A\) so that it may be instantiated at the triple-overlap rings of the
1179	  Grassmannian glue datum.
1180	\end{definition}
1181	
1182	\begin{lemma}
1183	
1184	\leanok
1185	[Left leg of the pullback isomorphism]
1186	  \label{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst}
1187	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayPullbackIso_inv_fst}
1188	  \uses{def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_away_incl_left}
1189	  Under the identification of \cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso}, the first
1190	  projection of the pullback corresponds to the left away-inclusion: the
1191	  composite
1192	  \(\Spec A[1/(xy)] \to \Spec A[1/x] \times_{\Spec A} \Spec A[1/y]
1193	    \xrightarrow{\mathrm{pr}_1} \Spec A[1/x]\)
1194	  equals \(\Spec\) of the inclusion \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y}\)
1195	  (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}).
1196	\end{lemma}
1197	\begin{proof}
1198	  \uses{def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_away_incl_left}
1199	  \leanok
1200	  The pullback-projection compatibility of the tensor-product identification
1201	  sends \(\mathrm{pr}_1\) to the left tensor inclusion, which under the
1202	  localisation isomorphism is the structure map \(A[1/x] \to A[1/(xy)]\), i.e.\
1203	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y}\); the verification is the universal property of the
1204	  away-localisation. Proved directly in Lean.
1205	\end{proof}
1206	
1207	\begin{lemma}
1208	
1209	\leanok
1210	[Right leg of the pullback isomorphism]
1211	  \label{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd}
1212	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayPullbackIso_inv_snd}
1213	  \uses{def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_away_incl_right}
1214	  Symmetrically, the second projection of the pullback corresponds to the right
1215	  away-inclusion: the composite
1216	  \(\Spec A[1/(xy)] \to \Spec A[1/x] \times_{\Spec A} \Spec A[1/y]
1217	    \xrightarrow{\mathrm{pr}_2} \Spec A[1/y]\)
1218	  equals \(\Spec\) of \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{x,y}\)
1219	  (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right}).
1220	\end{lemma}
1221	\begin{proof}
1222	  \uses{def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_away_incl_right}
1223	  \leanok
1224	  As in \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst}, with the right tensor inclusion in
1225	  place of the left. Proved directly in Lean.
1226	\end{proof}
1227	
1228	\begin{definition}
1229	
1230	\leanok
1231	[Order-swap isomorphism \(A[1/(xy)] \cong A[1/(yx)]\)]
1232	  \label{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}
1233	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayMulCommEquiv}
1234	  \uses{lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}
1235	  For \(x, y \in A\), the canonical ring isomorphism
1236	  \[
1237	    A[1/(xy)] \;\cong\; A[1/(yx)]
1238	  \]
1239	  identifying the two away-localisations, which agree because \(xy\) and \(yx\)
1240	  generate the same submonoid of \(A\)
1241	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}). It is inserted into the
1242	  triple-cocycle field of the glue datum to reconcile the opposite product
1243	  orders produced by the two pullback isomorphisms.
1244	\end{definition}
1245	
1246	\begin{lemma}
1247	
1248	\leanok
1249	[The order-swap lies over the base ring]
1250	  \label{lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap}
1251	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap}
1252	  \uses{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}
1253	  The order-swap isomorphism (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}) intertwines the
1254	  two structure maps: for \(x, y \in A\),
1255	  \[
1256	    \mathrm{swap}_{x,y} \circ \bigl(A \to A[1/(xy)]\bigr)
1257	      \;=\; \bigl(A \to A[1/(yx)]\bigr).
1258	  \]
1259	\end{lemma}
1260	\begin{proof}
1261	  \uses{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}
1262	  \leanok
1263	  Both \(A[1/(xy)]\) and \(A[1/(yx)]\) are localisations of \(A\) at the same
1264	  submonoid (\(xy\) and \(yx\) generate it), and the canonical comparison map
1265	  between two such localisations is an \(A\)-algebra isomorphism
1266	  (\cref{lem:mathlib_isLocalization_algEquiv}); an \(A\)-algebra map commutes with
1267	  the structure maps. Proved directly in Lean.
1268	\end{proof}
1269	
1270	\begin{lemma}
1271	
1272	\leanok
1273	[The order-swap absorbs the left inclusion into the right one]
1274	  \label{lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft}
1275	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.awayMulCommEquiv_comp_awayInclLeft}
1276	  \uses{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_away_incl_right, lem:gr_awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, lem:mathlib_away_lift}
1277	  For \(x, y \in A\), pre-composing the order-swap isomorphism
1278	  \(\mathrm{swap}_{x,y} : A[1/(xy)] \cong A[1/(yx)]\)
1279	  (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}) with the left away-inclusion
1280	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y} : A[1/x] \to A[1/(xy)]\)
1281	  (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}) yields the right away-inclusion of the swapped
1282	  pair:
1283	  \[
1284	    \mathrm{swap}_{x,y} \circ \iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{x,y}
1285	      \;=\; \iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{y,x}
1286	      \;:\; A[1/x] \longrightarrow A[1/(yx)] ,
1287	  \]
1288	  where \(\iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{y,x} : A[1/x] \to A[1/(yx)]\) is the right
1289	  away-inclusion (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_right}) for the pair \((y, x)\). This is
1290	  the order-swap coherence used to rotate the triple-overlap transitions.
1291	\end{lemma}
1292	\begin{proof}
1293	  \uses{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_away_incl_right, lem:gr_awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, lem:mathlib_away_lift}
1294	  \leanok
1295	  Both sides are ring maps out of the away-localisation \(A[1/x]\), so by the
1296	  universal property of the localisation -- the uniqueness of an extension along
1297	  the structure map \(A \to A[1/x]\) (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) -- it suffices
1298	  to check equality after pre-composing with that structure map, i.e.\ on the base
1299	  ring \(A\). There all three away-maps collapse to structure maps into
1300	  \(A[1/(yx)]\): the left inclusion by
1301	  \cref{lem:gr_awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap}, the order-swap by
1302	  \cref{lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap}, and the right inclusion by
1303	  \cref{lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap}. The two composites therefore agree
1304	  on \(A\), hence everywhere. Proved directly in Lean.
1305	\end{proof}

1307	\subsection*{The triple-overlap glue field \(t'\)}
1308	
1309	The scheme-gluing machine requires, beyond the pairwise transitions \(t_{I,J}\), a
1310	\emph{triple-overlap} field \(t'\) on the relevant fibre products, together with
1311	its compatibility \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) with the chart projections. We record both
1312	as the next layer of bridge results; their cocycle coherence is the residual
1313	obligation discharged in \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}.
1314	
1315	\begin{definition}
1316	
1317	\leanok
1318	[The triple-overlap transition \(t'\)]
1319	  \label{def:gr_chart_transition'}
1320	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartTransition'}
1321	  \uses{def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}
1322	  The \(t'\)-field of the Grassmannian glue datum is the morphism of schemes
1323	  \[
1324	    t'_{I,J,K} : U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K \longrightarrow U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I ,
1325	  \]
1326	  defined as the composite
1327	  \[
1328	    U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K
1329	      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } \Spec S_I
1330	      \xrightarrow{\ \Spec \Theta_{I,J}\ } \Spec S_J
1331	      \xrightarrow{\ \Spec \mathrm{swap}\ } \Spec R^J[1/(P^J_K P^J_I)]
1332	      \xrightarrow{\ \sim\ } U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I ,
1333	  \]
1334	  where the first and last arrows are the pullback identifications of
1335	  \cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso} -- the source one for the pair \((P^I_J, P^I_K)\)
1336	  over \(R^I\), and the target one (taken inverse) for the pair \((P^J_K, P^J_I)\)
1337	  over \(R^J\) -- the middle arrow is the \(\Spec\) of the localised
1338	  triple-overlap transition \(\Theta_{I,J} : S_J \to S_I\)
1339	  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}), and \(\mathrm{swap}\) is the \(\Spec\) of the
1340	  order-swap isomorphism \(R^J[1/(P^J_K P^J_I)] \cong S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]\)
1341	  (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}). The order-swap is inserted precisely to
1342	  reconcile the product order \(P^J_K P^J_I\) carried by the target pullback
1343	  identification with the order \(P^J_I P^J_K\) of the codomain \(S_J\) of
1344	  \(\Theta_{I,J}\).
1345	\end{definition}
1346	
1347	\begin{lemma}
1348	
1349	\leanok
1350	[The \(t'\) ring identity]
1351	  \label{lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity}
1352	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartTransition'_ringIdentity}
1353	  \uses{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_away_incl_right, def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, lem:mathlib_away_lift}
1354	  Over the triple-overlap rings, the localised transition \(\Theta_{I,J}\)
1355	  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) pre-composed with the order-swap and the right
1356	  inclusion equals the left inclusion post-composed with the plain transition
1357	  \(\theta_{I,J}\), as ring homomorphisms \(R^J[1/P^J_I] \to S_I\):
1358	  \[
1359	    \Theta_{I,J} \circ \mathrm{swap} \circ \iota^{\mathrm{R}}_{P^J_K, P^J_I}
1360	      \;=\; \iota^{\mathrm{L}}_{P^I_J, P^I_K} \circ \theta_{I,J} .
1361	  \]
1362	\end{lemma}
1363	\begin{proof}
1364	  \uses{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_away_incl_right, def:gr_away_incl_left, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap, lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap, lem:mathlib_away_lift}
1365	  \leanok
1366	  Both sides are ring maps out of the away-localisation \(R^J[1/P^J_I]\), so by the
1367	  universal property of the localisation (\cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}) it suffices
1368	  to check equality after pre-composing with the structure map
1369	  \(R^J \to R^J[1/P^J_I]\), i.e.\ on the chart ring \(R^J\). There the right
1370	  inclusion and the order-swap collapse to structure maps
1371	  (\cref{lem:gr_awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap},
1372	  \cref{lem:gr_awayMulCommEquiv_comp_algebraMap}), and the lift defining
1373	  \(\Theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) unwinds to
1374	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}} \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\)
1375	  (\cref{def:gr_away_incl_left}); the right side likewise unwinds through the lift
1376	  defining \(\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition}) to the same
1377	  \(\iota^{\mathrm{L}} \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\). Proved directly in Lean.
1378	\end{proof}
1379	
1380	\begin{lemma}
1381	
1382	\leanok
1383	[The \(t'\) compatibility field \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\)]
1384	  \label{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}
1385	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartTransition'_fac}
1386	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity, def:gr_away_pullback_iso, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd, def:gr_chart_transition}
1387	  The triple-overlap transition \(t'\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}) is
1388	  compatible with the projections to the charts:
1389	  \[
1390	    \mathrm{pr}_2 \circ t'_{I,J,K} \;=\; t_{I,J} \circ \mathrm{pr}_1 ,
1391	  \]
1392	  where \(\mathrm{pr}_1\) is the first projection of the source pullback
1393	  \(U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\), \(\mathrm{pr}_2\) the second projection of the
1394	  target pullback \(U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I\), and \(t_{I,J}\) the scheme-level
1395	  transition (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}).
1396	\end{lemma}
1397	\begin{proof}
1398	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity, def:gr_away_pullback_iso, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd, def:gr_chart_transition}
1399	  \leanok
1400	  Both fibre products are affine, so the identity may be checked after applying
1401	  \(\Spec\), as an identity of the underlying ring homomorphisms. Rewriting the
1402	  source projection \(\mathrm{pr}_1\) through the pullback identification
1403	  (\cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst}) and the target projection
1404	  \(\mathrm{pr}_2\) through \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd} turns the claim
1405	  into the ring identity \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity}: the three
1406	  localised pieces of \(t'\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}) collapse to the
1407	  left inclusion followed by the plain transition \(t_{I,J}\)
1408	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}). Proved directly in Lean.
1409	\end{proof}
1410	
1411	\begin{lemma}
1412	
1413	\leanok
1414	[The triple-overlap cocycle coherence]
1415	  \label{lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}
1416	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartTransition'_cocycle}
1417	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id, def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}
1418	  The triple-overlap transitions \(t'\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}) satisfy
1419	  the cocycle coherence on the source triple overlap \(U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K\):
1420	  \[
1421	    t'_{I,J,K} \;\circ\; t'_{J,K,I} \;\circ\; t'_{K,I,J} \;=\; \mathrm{id}.
1422	  \]
1423	  This is the \(\mathrm{cocycle}\) field of the Grassmannian glue datum -- the
1424	  categorical realisation of the rotated ring identity \(\Phi = \id\)
1425	  (\cref{lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id}).
1426	\end{lemma}
1427	\begin{proof}
1428	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id, def:gr_away_pullback_iso, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}
1429	  \leanok
1430	  Expand each factor \(t'\) into its constituents
1431	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}): a source pullback identification, the
1432	  \(\Spec\) of a localised triple-overlap transition \(\Theta\)
1433	  (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}) pre-composed with the \(\Spec\) of an
1434	  order-swap (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}), and an inverse target pullback
1435	  identification. In the threefold composite the two internal pairs
1436	  \(\mathrm{ap}^{-1} \circ \mathrm{ap}\) of pullback identifications
1437	  (\cref{def:gr_away_pullback_iso}) cancel, adjacent factors sharing the same
1438	  identification. There remain the six \(\Spec\)-arrows of the three \(\Theta\)'s
1439	  and three order-swaps; these collapse into a single \(\Spec\) of their composite
1440	  ring endomorphism \(\Phi\). By \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id} one has
1441	  \(\Phi = \id\), so this middle \(\Spec\) is the identity, and the outer
1442	  conjugating pullback isomorphism cancels against its inverse, leaving the
1443	  identity of the source triple overlap. Proved directly in Lean.
1444	\end{proof}
1445	
1446	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1447	[The rotated triple-overlap ring cocycle identity \(\Phi = \id\)]
1448	  \label{lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id}
1449	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.cocyclePhiId}
1450	  \uses{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij, def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
1451	    lem:gr_cocycle}
1452	  \textit{The ring identity underlying the \(\mathrm{cocycle}\) field of the glue datum.}
1453	  Fix \(d \le r\) and three subsets \(I,J,K \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) of cardinality \(d\). On the
1454	  triple-overlap chart ring \(R_{IJK} := \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,(P^I_J\cdot P^I_K)\)
1455	  the composite ring endomorphism
1456	  \[
1457	    \Phi := \Theta_{I,J,K} \circ \mathrm{swap}_J \circ \Theta_{J,K,I} \circ \mathrm{swap}_K
1458	            \circ \Theta_{K,I,J} \circ \mathrm{swap}_I
1459	  \]
1460	  equals the identity \(\mathrm{RingHom.id}\,R_{IJK}\), where each \(\Theta_{X,Y,Z} =
1461	  \mathtt{cocycleΘIJ}\,d\,r\,X\,Y\,Z\) (\cref{def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij}, taken for the rotated index
1462	  triple) and each \(\mathrm{swap}_X\) is the matching order-swap algebra equivalence
1463	  \(\mathtt{awayMulCommEquiv}\) (\cref{def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv}).
1464	\end{lemma}
1465	\begin{proof}
1466	  \uses{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq, lem:gr_cocycle}
1467	  \leanok
1468	  Both endomorphisms are ring homomorphisms out of the localization
1469	  \(R_{IJK} = \mathrm{Localization.Away}\,(P^I_J\cdot P^I_K)\), so by Mathlib's
1470	  \(\mathrm{IsLocalization.ringHom\_ext}\) for the submonoid generated by
1471	  \(P^I_J\cdot P^I_K\) it suffices to
1472	  check equality on the chart-ring generators (the images of the universal matrix entries). On those
1473	  generators \(\Phi\) unwinds, via the order-swaps and the \(\Theta\) definitions, to the rotated
1474	  analogue of the matrix cocycle collapse \((Y_K)^{-1}Y\) already proved in
1475	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq} — the same image-matrix identity that underlies the scheme-level
1476	  cocycle condition \cref{lem:gr_cocycle}. Reusing that computation for the rotated triple shows each
1477	  generator is fixed, whence \(\Phi = \id\).
1478	\end{proof}
1479	
1480	\section{The glued Grassmannian scheme}
1481	\label{sec:gr_glued}
1482	
1483	\begin{definition}
1484	
1485	\leanok
1486	[The Grassmannian glue datum]
1487	  \label{def:gr_the_glue_data}
1488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.theGlueData}
1489	  \uses{def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_chart_overlap, def:gr_chart_incl,
1490	    def:gr_chart_transition, def:gr_chart_transition',
1491	    lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion, lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso,
1492	    lem:gr_chartTransition_self, lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac,
1493	    lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}
1494	  The \emph{Grassmannian glue datum} is the \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) bundle
1495	  indexed by the set \(\{\, I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\} : \#(I) = d \,\}\) of
1496	  size-\(d\) subsets, assembling the data constructed above:
1497	  \begin{itemize}
1498	    \item objects \(U := U^I\), the affine charts (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart});
1499	    \item overlaps \(V := U^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap});
1500	    \item inclusions \(f := \bigl(U^I_J \hookrightarrow U^I\bigr)\)
1501	      (\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}), which are open immersions
1502	      (\cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}), with the self-inclusion an
1503	      isomorphism (the \(f_{\mathrm{id}}\) field,
1504	      \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso});
1505	    \item transitions \(t := t_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}), with
1506	      \(t_{\mathrm{id}}\) field \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self};
1507	    \item the triple-overlap field \(t' := t'_{I,J,K}\)
1508	      (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}), with compatibility field
1509	      \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}) and \(\mathrm{cocycle}\)
1510	      field (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}).
1511	  \end{itemize}
1512	  The remaining structural fields \(f_{\mathrm{mono}}\) (each inclusion is a
1513	  monomorphism) and \(f_{\mathrm{hasPullback}}\) (the relevant fibre products
1514	  exist) are discharged by instance synthesis from the open-immersion property of
1515	  the inclusions. Assembling these named fields into the
1516	  \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) record yields the datum from which the glued scheme
1517	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is formed.
1518	\end{definition}
1519	
1520	\begin{definition}
1521	
1522	\leanok
1523	[The Grassmannian scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
1524	  \label{def:gr_glued_scheme}
1525	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.scheme}
1526	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_cocycle,
1527	    def:gr_chart_overlap, def:gr_chart_incl, lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion,
1528	    lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso, def:gr_chart_transition,
1529	    lem:gr_chartTransition_self, def:gr_away_pullback_iso,
1530	    lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst, lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd,
1531	    def:gr_away_mul_comm_equiv, def:gr_cocycle_theta_ij,
1532	    def:gr_chart_transition', lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac,
1533	    lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity, lem:gr_cocycle_imageMatrix_eq,
1534	    lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id, lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle}
1535	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Construction by gluing together
1536	  % affine patches" (read from
1537	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L843-L850).
1538	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1539	  % "Therefore the schemes
1540	  %  $U^I$, as $I$ varies over all the
1541	  %  ${r\choose d}$ different
1542	  %  subsets of  $\{ 1,\ldots, r\}$ of cardinality $d$,
1543	  %  can be glued together by the co-cycle $(\theta_{I,J})$ to form a
1544	  %  finite-type scheme $\grass(r,d)$ over $\Z$. As each $U^I$ is
1545	  %  isomorphic to $\AA^{d(r-d)}_{\Z}$, it follows that
1546	  %  $\grass(r,d) \to \spec \Z$ is smooth of relative dimension $d(r-d)$."
1547	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1548	  Gluing the affine charts \(\{U^I\}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}), one for each
1549	  of the \(\binom{r}{d}\) subsets \(I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\}\) of cardinality
1550	  \(d\), along the open subschemes \(U^I_J \subseteq U^I\) via the transition
1551	  isomorphisms \(\theta_{I,J} : U^I_J \xrightarrow{\sim} U^J_I\)
1552	  (\cref{def:gr_transition}) -- which satisfy the cocycle condition
1553	  \(\theta_{I,K} = \theta_{J,K} \circ \theta_{I,J}\) and \(\theta_{I,I} = \id\)
1554	  of \cref{lem:gr_cocycle} -- yields a scheme
1555	  \[
1556	    \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \longrightarrow \Spec \mathbb{Z}.
1557	  \]
1558	  It is of finite type over \(\mathbb{Z}\) (a finite gluing of finitely
1559	  generated \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebras), and under the gluing each \(U^I\) is an
1560	  open subscheme of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) with \(U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J\).
1561	
1562	  \emph{Construction of the glue datum.} The gluing is carried out through the
1563	  Mathlib vehicle for scheme gluing, a glue datum in the sense of a category of
1564	  schemes (the structure \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\), extending the abstract
1565	  glue-datum interface). Its data are supplied as follows. The index set is the
1566	  set \(J := \{\, I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\} : \#(I) = d \,\}\) of size-\(d\)
1567	  subsets. The objects are the charts \(U^I\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}); the
1568	  pairwise overlaps are the schemes \(U^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}); the
1569	  inclusions \(f_{I,J} : U^I_J \to U^I\) are the open immersions
1570	  \(\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}\) (open immersions by
1571	  \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}, in particular monomorphisms), with the
1572	  identity field \(f_{\mathrm{id}}\) supplied by
1573	  \cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_self_isIso}; the transition isomorphisms
1574	  \(t_{I,J} : U^I_J \to U^J_I\) are the scheme-level transitions
1575	  \(\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}\), with identity field \(t_{\mathrm{id}}\)
1576	  supplied by \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self}.
1577	
1578	  The triple-overlap field \(t'\) and its first coherence obligation are supplied
1579	  by the two preceding results. The field itself is the morphism
1580	  \(t'_{I,J,K} : U^I_J \times_{U^I} U^I_K \to U^J_K \times_{U^J} U^J_I\) of
1581	  \cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}, and the compatibility field
1582	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}}\) -- that \(t'\) is compatible with the projections to the
1583	  charts -- is \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}, which reduces (both fibre
1584	  products being affine) to the ring identity
1585	  \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_ringIdentity} via the leg lemmas
1586	  \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_fst} and \cref{lem:gr_awayPullbackIso_inv_snd}.
1587	
1588	  \emph{Cocycle field.} The \(t'\)-cocycle coherence
1589	  \(t'_{I,J,K} \circ t'_{J,K,I} \circ t'_{K,I,J} = \mathrm{id}\)
1590	  follows from \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_cocycle} via the ring identity
1591	  \cref{lem:gr_cocycle_phi_id}. With this field in hand the glue datum is
1592	  complete: it is the structure \(\mathtt{Scheme.GlueData}\) with
1593	  \(U := \texttt{affineChart}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}),
1594	  \(V := \texttt{chartOverlap}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}),
1595	  \(f := \texttt{chartIncl}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}),
1596	  \(t := \texttt{chartTransition}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}),
1597	  \(t_{\mathrm{id}} := \texttt{chartTransition\_self}\)
1598	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_self}),
1599	  \(t' := \texttt{chartTransition'}\) (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition'}),
1600	  \(t_{\mathrm{fac}} := \texttt{chartTransition'\_fac}\)
1601	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartTransition'_fac}), and the cocycle field above. The glued
1602	  scheme \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is then the colimit
1603	  \((\text{glue datum}).\mathrm{glued}\) produced by the gluing machine.
1604	
1605	  Moreover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is \emph{smooth of relative
1606	  dimension \(d(r-d)\)}. Smoothness and relative dimension are local on the
1607	  source; the charts \(\{U^I\}\) cover \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and each
1608	  \(U^I \cong \mathbb{A}^{d(r-d)}_{\mathbb{Z}}\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) is
1609	  affine space of dimension \(d(r-d)\) over \(\mathbb{Z}\), which is smooth over
1610	  \(\mathbb{Z}\) of relative dimension \(d(r-d)\) (the polynomial ring
1611	  \(\mathbb{Z}[x_1, \dots, x_{d(r-d)}]\) is a smooth \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra). As
1612	  the property may be checked on an open cover of the source, the conclusion
1613	  follows.
1614	\end{definition}
1615	
1616	\section{Separatedness}
1617	\label{sec:gr_separated}
1618	
1619	The surjectivity of the restricted-diagonal comorphism is the ring-theoretic
1620	heart of separatedness. We isolate it through the following sequence of
1621	helper results before stating the keystone lemma.
1622	
1623	\begin{lemma}
1624	
1625	\leanok
1626	[Two-sided inverse identity \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\,P^I_J = 1\)]
1627	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1628	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1629	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1630	  In \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), the pre-localisation transition hom
1631	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) satisfies the explicit
1632	  two-sided inverse identity
1633	  \[
1634	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_I\bigr) \cdot P^I_J \;=\; 1 .
1635	  \]
1636	  This refines the unit assertion of \cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit} to a named
1637	  inverse: \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the explicit inverse of \(P^I_J\) in
1638	  \(R^I_J\).
1639	\end{lemma}
1640	\begin{proof}
1641	  \uses{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, lem:gr_transition_pre_unit, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}
1642	  \leanok
1643	  By \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet} (taking the third subset \(K = I\)),
1644	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\) is the determinant of the \(I\)-minor of the image
1645	  matrix \(M = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\), which by
1646	  \cref{lem:gr_universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap} equals
1647	  \(\det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I\bigr) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\). Hence
1648	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\), and multiplying by \(P^I_J =
1649	  \det(X^I_J)\) gives \(\det(X^I_J)^{-1} \cdot \det(X^I_J) = 1\) by the
1650	  localisation-inverse cancellation for the unit \(\det(X^I_J)\)
1651	  (\cref{lem:gr_transition_pre_unit}).
1652	\end{proof}
1653	
1654	\begin{definition}
1655	
1656	\leanok
1657	[Restricted-diagonal comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1658	  \label{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1659	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap}
1660	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre}
1661	  The \emph{restricted-diagonal comorphism} on the patch \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
1662	  U^J\) is the \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism
1663	  \[
1664	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
1665	    \longrightarrow R^I_J,
1666	    \qquad
1667	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad
1668	    1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
1669	  \]
1670	  whose surjectivity is the content of separatedness; this is the \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1671	  of \cref{lem:gr_separated}.
1672	\end{definition}
1673	\begin{proof}
1674	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre}
1675	  \leanok
1676	  Construct \(\delta_{I,J}\) as the tensor-product lift of the two commuting
1677	  factors: the structure map \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] = R^I \to R^I_J\) on the left
1678	  factor, and the pre-localisation transition hom \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\)
1679	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) on the right factor. Since both land in the
1680	  commutative ring \(R^I_J\), the universal property of the tensor product of
1681	  \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebras yields a unique \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra homomorphism out
1682	  of \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\) restricting to these
1683	  factors; by construction its right component is \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\).
1684	\end{proof}
1685	
1686	\begin{lemma}
1687	
1688	\leanok
1689	[Left component of \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1690	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left}
1691	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_left}
1692	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1693	  For \(a \in \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\), the comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1694	  (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) acts on the left factor by the structure map:
1695	  \[
1696	    \delta_{I,J}(a \otimes 1) \;=\; \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a),
1697	  \]
1698	  the image of \(a\) under \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] = R^I \to R^I_J\).
1699	\end{lemma}
1700	\begin{proof}
1701	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1702	  \leanok
1703	  Immediate from the defining tensor-product lift of
1704	  \cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}: the value on a left elementary tensor \(a \otimes
1705	  1\) is the left factor evaluated at \(a\), i.e.\ the structure map.
1706	\end{proof}
1707	
1708	\begin{lemma}
1709	
1710	\leanok
1711	[Right component of \(\delta_{I,J}\)]
1712	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right}
1713	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_right}
1714	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1715	  For \(b \in \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\), the comorphism \(\delta_{I,J}\)
1716	  (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) acts on the right factor by the pre-localisation
1717	  transition hom:
1718	  \[
1719	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes b) \;=\; \tilde\theta_{I,J}(b)
1720	  \]
1721	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}).
1722	\end{lemma}
1723	\begin{proof}
1724	  \uses{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}
1725	  \leanok
1726	  Immediate from the defining tensor-product lift of
1727	  \cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}: the value on a right elementary tensor \(1 \otimes
1728	  b\) is the right factor \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) evaluated at \(b\).
1729	\end{proof}
1730	
1731	\begin{lemma}
1732	
1733	\leanok
1734	[The restricted-diagonal comorphism is surjective]
1735	  \label{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1736	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.diagonalRingMap_surjective}
1737	  \uses{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1738	  The comorphism \(\delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}}
1739	  \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to R^I_J\) (\cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}) is surjective.
1740	\end{lemma}
1741	\begin{proof}
1742	  \uses{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
1743	  \leanok
1744	  Let \(z \in R^I_J\). Since \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) is the
1745	  localisation of \(R^I = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) away from \(P^I_J\), surjectivity of
1746	  the localisation map provides \(a \in R^I\) and \(n \in \mathbb{N}\) with
1747	  \[
1748	    z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \;=\; \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a).
1749	  \]
1750	  Consider the witness \(a \otimes (P^J_I)^n \in \mathbb{Z}[X^I]
1751	  \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]\). Using multiplicativity together with
1752	  \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_left} and \cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_right},
1753	  \[
1754	    \delta_{I,J}\bigl(a \otimes (P^J_I)^n\bigr)
1755	      = \mathrm{alg}_{R^I \to R^I_J}(a) \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1756	      = z \cdot (P^I_J)^n \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)^n
1757	      = z \cdot \bigl(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I)\bigr)^n
1758	      = z,
1759	  \]
1760	  where the last equality uses \(P^I_J \cdot \tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_I) = 1\) from
1761	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}. Hence \(z\) lies in the image
1762	  of \(\delta_{I,J}\), so \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective.
1763	\end{proof}
1764	
1765	\begin{definition}
1766	
1767	\leanok
1768	[Source pullback iso \(e_2\) for the diagonal computation]
1769	  \label{def:gr_pullbackιIso}
1770	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.pullbackιIso}
1771	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chart_overlap}
1772	  The \emph{source pullback iso} \(e_2\) identifies the categorical fibre product
1773	  of two chart inclusions of the glued scheme (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) with
1774	  the overlap chart:
1775	  \[
1776	    e_2 : \mathrm{pullback}\,(\iota_i)\,(\iota_j) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
1777	    U^I \cap U^J \;=\; \texttt{chartOverlap}\,(d,r,i,j)
1778	  \]
1779	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_overlap}), where \(\iota_i, \iota_j\) are the open
1780	  immersions of the charts \(U^I, U^J\) into \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\).
1781	\end{definition}
1782	\begin{proof}
1783	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_chart_overlap}
1784	  \leanok
1785	  The glue data of \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} supplies, for each pair \((i,j)\), a
1786	  pullback cone over \(\iota_i, \iota_j\) with apex the overlap chart \(U^I \cap
1787	  U^J\), and this cone is a limit cone. Comparing it with the categorical
1788	  pullback limit cone via the uniqueness-up-to-isomorphism of limit cone points
1789	  yields the desired isomorphism \(e_2\).
1790	\end{proof}
1791	
1792	\begin{definition}
1793	
1794	\leanok
1795	[Structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)]
1796	  \label{def:gr_to_specZ}
1797	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.toSpecZ}
1798	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme}
1799	  The \emph{structure morphism} of the glued Grassmannian scheme
1800	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is the canonical map
1801	  \[
1802	    \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \longrightarrow \Spec \mathbb{Z} .
1803	  \]
1804	  As \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) lives in the category of schemes over \(\mathbb{Z}\), in
1805	  which \(\Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is the terminal object, this morphism is the unique
1806	  map to that terminal object. It is the genuine base over which separatedness and
1807	  properness of the Grassmannian are formulated.
1808	\end{definition}
1809	
1810	\begin{lemma}
1811	
1812	\leanok
1813	[The chart map composes to the affine structure map]
1814	  \label{lem:gr_ι_toSpecZ}
1815	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.ι_toSpecZ}
1816	  \uses{def:gr_to_specZ, def:gr_glued_scheme}

1427	ingredients over `toSpecZ`:
1428	* `QuasiCompact` — from the finite affine chart cover (`compactSpace_scheme`);
1429	* `QuasiSeparated` — free from `isSeparatedToSpecZ` (`[IsSeparated f] : QuasiSeparated f`);
1430	* `LocallyOfFiniteType` — each chart map `ℤ → R^I = ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]` is of finite type;

1444	  haveI : Finite (theGlueData d r).openCover.I₀ :=
1445	    inferInstanceAs (Finite {I : Finset (Fin r) // I.card = d})
1446	  haveI : ∀ i, CompactSpace ((theGlueData d r).openCover.X i) := fun i =>
1447	    inferInstanceAs (CompactSpace
1448	      (Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ i.1}) ℤ))))
1449	  exact (theGlueData d r).openCover.compactSpace

1495	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
1496	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
1497	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK) *
1498	        algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1499	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
1500	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
1501	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I K hI hK) := by

1503	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
1504	        (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))
1505	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
1506	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
1507	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
1508	  rw [hsub, Matrix.det_mul]

1510	          (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)))).submatrix id
1511	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det

1513	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]; exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
1514	  rw [hdet]

1516	      = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
1517	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm

1541	The existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1, "Properness") is

1548	(`Spec.preimage`).

1559	theorem existence_chart_factorization (d r : ℕ) {K : Type} [Field K]
1560	    (i₁ : Spec (CommRingCat.of K) ⟶ scheme d r) :
1561	    ∃ (I : (theGlueData d r).J)

1564	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ : Nonempty (↥(Spec (CommRingCat.of K))) := inferInstance
1565	  obtain ⟨I, y, hy⟩ := (theGlueData d r).ι_jointly_surjective (i₁.base x₀)
1566	  have hrange : Set.range i₁.base ⊆ Set.range ((theGlueData d r).ι I).base := by
1567	    rintro z ⟨x', rfl⟩
1568	    rw [Subsingleton.elim x' x₀]

1572	    (@IsOpenImmersion.lift _ _ _ ((theGlueData d r).ι I) i₁ hoi hrange)).hom, ?_⟩

1583	theorem existence_minimal_valuation (d r : ℕ)

1585	    [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K]
1586	    (I : (theGlueData d r).J)

1594	  haveI : Finite (theGlueData d r).J :=

1682	    fun k => if k = p then q else (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) with hcm
1683	  -- `colMap k ∈ K'`.
1684	  have hmem : ∀ k, colMap k ∈ K' := by
1685	    intro k
1686	    simp only [hcm]
1687	    by_cases hk : k = p
1688	    · rw [if_pos hk, hK'def]; exact Finset.mem_insert_self _ _
1689	    · rw [if_neg hk, hK'def, Finset.mem_insert]
1690	      refine Or.inr ?_
1691	      rw [Finset.mem_erase]
1692	      refine ⟨?_, (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2⟩
1693	      rw [hjp]; intro hcontra
1694	      exact hk ((hJinj k p).mp hcontra)
1695	  -- Injectivity of `colMap`.
1696	  have hcmInj : Function.Injective colMap := by
1697	    intro k k' hkk'
1698	    simp only [hcm] at hkk'
1699	    by_cases hk : k = p <;> by_cases hk' : k' = p
1700	    · rw [hk, hk']
1701	    · exfalso
1702	      rw [if_pos hk, if_neg hk'] at hkk'
1703	      exact hq (hkk' ▸ (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k').2)
1704	    · exfalso
1705	      rw [if_neg hk, if_pos hk'] at hkk'
1706	      exact hq (hkk'.symm ▸ (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2)
1707	    · rw [if_neg hk, if_neg hk'] at hkk'
1708	      exact (hJinj k k').mp hkk'
1709	  -- The order-reindexing permutation `σ` with `oiK' ∘ σ = colMap`.
1710	  set oiK' : Fin d → Fin r := fun k => (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK' k : Fin r) with hoiK'
1711	  set σfun : Fin d → Fin d :=
1712	    fun k => (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK').symm ⟨colMap k, hmem k⟩ with hσfun
1713	  have hσInj : Function.Injective σfun := by
1714	    intro k k' h
1715	    simp only [hσfun] at h
1716	    have h2 : (⟨colMap k, hmem k⟩ : ↥K') = ⟨colMap k', hmem k'⟩ :=
1717	      (K'.orderIsoOfFin hK').symm.injective h
1718	    exact hcmInj (Subtype.mk_eq_mk.mp h2)
1719	  set σ : Equiv.Perm (Fin d) :=
1720	    Equiv.ofBijective σfun (Finite.injective_iff_bijective.mp hσInj) with hσ
1721	  have hσprop : ∀ k, oiK' (σ k) = colMap k := by
1722	    intro k
1723	    simp only [hoiK', hσ, Equiv.ofBijective_apply, hσfun]
1724	    rw [OrderIso.apply_symm_apply]
1725	  -- The substituted-identity matrix and its determinant.
1726	  set v : Fin d → MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ :=
1727	    fun k' => universalMatrix d r J hJ k' q with hv
1728	  have hBupd : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap
1729	      = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d)
1730	          (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)).updateCol p v := by
1731	    apply Matrix.ext
1732	    intro k' k
1733	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, Matrix.updateCol_apply]
1734	    simp only [hcm]
1735	    by_cases hk : k = p
1736	    · simp only [if_pos hk, hv]
1737	    · simp only [if_neg hk, universalMatrix]
1738	      rw [dif_pos (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k).2, Matrix.one_apply]
1739	      by_cases hkk : k' = k
1740	      · rw [if_pos ((hJinj k' k).mpr hkk), if_pos hkk]
1741	      · rw [if_neg (fun h => hkk ((hJinj k' k).mp h)), if_neg hkk]
1742	  have hdetB : ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap).det
1743	      = MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩) := by
1744	    rw [hBupd, det_one_updateCol]
1745	    simp only [hv, universalMatrix, dif_neg hq]
1746	  -- Relate `colMap` to the `K'`-minor via the permutation `σ`.
1747	  have hcolEq : oiK' ∘ σ = colMap := funext hσprop
1748	  have hsubEq : (universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id colMap
1749	      = ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id oiK').submatrix id (σ : Fin d → Fin d) := by
1750	    rw [Matrix.submatrix_submatrix, Function.comp_id, hcolEq]
1751	  have hminor : ((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id oiK').det = minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' := by
1752	    rw [minorDet]
1753	  have hsign : MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, hq⟩)
1754	      = (↑(↑(Equiv.Perm.sign σ) : ℤ) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ)
1755	        * minorDet d r J K' hJ hK' := by
1756	    rw [← hdetB, hsubEq, Matrix.det_permute', hminor]
1757	  rcases Int.units_eq_one_or (Equiv.Perm.sign σ) with hs | hs
1758	  · left
1759	    rw [hsign, hs]; simp
1760	  · right
1761	    rw [hsign, hs]; simp
