1763	/-- **E3 — entries land in `R`; `g` factors through the valuation ring**
1764	(`lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring`): for a valuation ring `R` with fraction
1765	field `K`, a chart index `I` with a `K`-point `f : R^I → K`, and the minimal-valuation chart
1766	index `J` (so `f(P^I_J)` is a unit and `v(f(P^I_{J'})) ≤ v(f(P^I_J))` for all `J'`), the
1767	composite `g := f' ∘ θ̃_{I,J}` (where `f' = lift f along P^I_J`) sends every element of
1768	`R^J = ℤ[X^J]` into the subring `(algebraMap R K).range ⊆ K`. Hence `g` factors through `R`.
1769	
1770	Proof: by `MvPolynomial` induction it suffices to check the constants (in `ℤ ⊆ R`) and the
1771	free generators `x^J_{p,q}`. Each generator equals `± P^J_{K'}` (`exists_minorDet_eq_free_entry`),
1772	and `g(P^J_{K'}) = f(P^I_{K'})/f(P^I_J)` has valuation `≤ 1` by the maximality of `J`, hence lies
1773	in `R = v.integer` (`ValuationRing.range_algebraMap_eq`). Project-local: the genuine geometric
1774	content of the existence half of the valuative criterion for `toSpecZ` (Nitsure §1). -/
1775	theorem existence_factor_through_valuationRing (d r : ℕ)
1776	    {R K : Type} [CommRing R] [IsDomain R] [ValuationRing R] [Field K]
1777	    [Algebra R K] [IsFractionRing R K]
1778	    (I J : (theGlueData d r).J)
1779	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I.1}) ℤ →+* K)
1780	    (hJmax : ∀ J' : (theGlueData d r).J,
1781	        ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J'.1 I.2 J'.2))
1782	          ≤ ValuationRing.valuation R K (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)))
1783	    (hf : IsUnit (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))) :
1784	    ∀ x : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J.1}) ℤ,
1785	      ((IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf).comp
1786	        (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom) x ∈ (algebraMap R K).range := by
1787	  set g : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J.1}) ℤ →+* K :=
1788	    (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf).comp
1789	      (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2).toRingHom with hg
1790	  have key : ∀ x, g x = (IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2) hf)
1791	      (transitionPreMap d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2 x) := fun _ => rfl
1792	  set v := ValuationRing.valuation R K with hv
1793	  -- Each value `g(P^J_{K'})` lies in `R`.
1794	  have hminorR : ∀ (K' : Finset (Fin r)) (hK' : K'.card = d),
1795	      g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK') ∈ (algebraMap R K).range := by
1796	    intro K' hK'
1797	    rw [← ValuationRing.range_algebraMap_eq R K, Valuation.mem_integer_iff]
1798	    have hmul : g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK') * f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)
1799	        = f (minorDet d r I.1 K' I.2 hK') := by
1800	      rw [key]
1801	      exact existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul d r I.1 J.1 K' I.2 J.2 hK' f hf
1802	    have hmax : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 K' I.2 hK'))
1803	        ≤ v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := hJmax ⟨K', hK'⟩
1804	    have hc : v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) ≠ 0 :=
1805	      (Valuation.ne_zero_iff v).mpr hf.ne_zero
1806	    have hvmul : v (g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK')) * v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))
1807	        ≤ v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := by
1808	      rw [← Valuation.map_mul, hmul]; exact hmax
1809	    have h' : v (g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK')) * v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2))
1810	        ≤ 1 * v (f (minorDet d r I.1 J.1 I.2 J.2)) := by rwa [one_mul]
1811	    exact le_of_mul_le_mul_right h' (zero_lt_iff.mpr hc)
1812	  -- The free generators land in `R` via the cofactor identity.
1813	  have hgen : ∀ n : Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J.1},
1814	      g (MvPolynomial.X n) ∈ (algebraMap R K).range := by
1815	    rintro ⟨pp, ⟨qq, hqq⟩⟩
1816	    obtain ⟨K', hK', hcase⟩ := exists_minorDet_eq_free_entry d r J.1 J.2 pp qq hqq
1817	    have hmemR := hminorR K' hK'
1818	    rcases hcase with h | h
1819	    · have hX : g (MvPolynomial.X (pp, ⟨qq, hqq⟩)) = g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK') := by
1820	        rw [h]
1821	      rw [hX]; exact hmemR
1822	    · have hX : g (MvPolynomial.X (pp, ⟨qq, hqq⟩)) = - g (minorDet d r J.1 K' J.2 hK') := by
1823	        rw [h, map_neg, neg_neg]
1824	      rw [hX]; exact neg_mem hmemR
1825	  -- Induction over `MvPolynomial`.
1826	  intro x
1827	  induction x using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
1828	  | C a =>
1829	    refine ⟨(a : R), ?_⟩
1830	    have h1 : g (MvPolynomial.C a) = (a : K) :=
1831	      RingHom.congr_fun (RingHom.ext_int (g.comp MvPolynomial.C) (Int.castRingHom K)) a
1832	    rw [map_intCast, h1]
1833	  | add p q hp hq => rw [map_add]; exact add_mem hp hq
1834	  | mul_X p n hp => rw [map_mul]; exact mul_mem hp (hgen n)
1835	
1836	end AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian

1862	\begin{lemma}
1863	
1864	\leanok
1865	[Second projection leg of the source pullback iso]
1866	  \label{lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_snd}
1867	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.pullbackιIso_inv_snd}
1868	  \uses{def:gr_pullbackιIso, def:gr_chart_incl, def:gr_chart_transition}
1869	  The inverse of the source pullback iso \(e_2\) (\cref{def:gr_pullbackιIso}),
1870	  followed by the second projection of the fibre product, is the scheme-level
1871	  transition composed with the opposite chart inclusion:
1872	  \[
1873	    e_2^{-1} \circ \mathrm{pr}_2
1874	      \;=\; t_{I,J} \circ \iota^J_I : U^I_J \to U^J_I \to U^J ,
1875	  \]
1876	  the transition \(t_{I,J}\) of \cref{def:gr_chart_transition} followed by the
1877	  chart inclusion of \cref{def:gr_chart_incl}.
1878	\end{lemma}
1879	\begin{proof}
1880	  \uses{def:gr_pullbackιIso, def:gr_chart_incl, def:gr_chart_transition}
1881	  \leanok
1882	  As in \cref{lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_fst}, but using the right leg of the cospan:
1883	  the glue-datum pullback cone's second leg is the composite \(t \circ f\) of the
1884	  glue datum (transition followed by inclusion), so the comparison isomorphism
1885	  identifies \(e_2^{-1} \circ \mathrm{pr}_2\) with \(t_{I,J} \circ \iota^J_I\).
1886	\end{proof}

1888	\begin{lemma}
1889	
1890	\leanok
1891	[The transition-then-inclusion composite is affine]
1892	  \label{lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
1893	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
1894	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition, def:gr_chart_incl, def:gr_transition_pre}
1895	  The composite of the scheme-level transition
1896	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_transition}) with the opposite chart inclusion
1897	  (\cref{def:gr_chart_incl}) is the \(\Spec\) of the pre-localisation transition
1898	  homomorphism \(\tilde\theta_{I,J} : R^J \to R^I[1/P^I_J]\)
1899	  (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}):
1900	  \[
1901	    t_{I,J} \circ \iota^J_I \;=\; \Spec \tilde\theta_{I,J} :
1902	      U^I_J \to U^J .
1903	  \]
1904	\end{lemma}
1905	\begin{proof}
1906	  \uses{def:gr_chart_transition, def:gr_chart_incl, def:gr_transition_pre}
1907	  \leanok
1908	  Unfolding the two scheme maps, the composite is \(\Spec\) of the ring map
1909	  obtained by precomposing the localised transition \(\theta_{I,J} :
1910	  R^J[1/P^J_I] \to R^I[1/P^I_J]\) with the localisation \(R^J \to R^J[1/P^J_I]\).
1911	  By the universal property of the away-localisation (the lift commutes with the
1912	  localisation map), this composite ring map is exactly the pre-localisation
1913	  transition \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\); applying \(\Spec\) and the contravariance of
1914	  composition gives the claim.
1915	\end{proof}

1917	\begin{lemma}
1918	
1919	\leanok
1920	[The structure morphism is separated (morphism form)]
1921	  \label{lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
1922	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparatedToSpecZ}
1923	  \uses{def:gr_to_specZ, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_pullbackιIso,
1924	    def:gr_diagonalRingMap, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective,
1925	    lem:gr_ι_toSpecZ, lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_fst,
1926	    lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_snd, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
1927	  The structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
1928	  (\cref{def:gr_to_specZ}) is separated: its diagonal is a closed immersion.
1929	\end{lemma}
1930	\begin{proof}
1931	  \uses{def:gr_to_specZ, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_pullbackιIso,
1932	    def:gr_diagonalRingMap, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective,
1933	    lem:gr_ι_toSpecZ, lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_fst,
1934	    lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_snd, lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
1935	    \leanok
1936	  Separatedness is local on the target of the diagonal, so it may be checked on
1937	  the cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \times_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) by the
1938	  patch products \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\) (the left--right product of the
1939	  chart open cover with itself). Fix a patch \((i,j)\). On it the restricted
1940	  diagonal is identified, via the source pullback iso \(e_2\)
1941	  (\cref{def:gr_pullbackιIso}) — whose two legs are computed in
1942	  \cref{lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_fst} and \cref{lem:gr_pullbackιIso_inv_snd},
1943	  using \cref{lem:gr_ι_toSpecZ} and \cref{lem:gr_chartTransition_comp_chartIncl}
1944	  to read off the affine comorphisms — and the target affine iso (the pullback of
1945	  two affine schemes is the \(\Spec\) of the tensor product), with the affine
1946	  morphism \(\Spec \delta_{I,J}\), where \(\delta_{I,J}\) is the
1947	  restricted-diagonal comorphism of \cref{def:gr_diagonalRingMap}. Since
1948	  \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective (\cref{lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective}), the
1949	  morphism \(\Spec \delta_{I,J}\) is a closed immersion. As every patch of the
1950	  diagonal is a closed immersion, the diagonal is a closed immersion and the
1951	  structure morphism is separated.
1952	\end{proof}

1954	\begin{lemma}
1955	
1956	\leanok
1957	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over \(\mathbb{Z}\)]
1958	  \label{lem:gr_separated}
1959	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isSeparated}
1960	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective, lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
1961	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Separatedness" (read from
1962	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860).
1963	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
1964	  % "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with $U^I\times U^J$
1965	  %  can be seen to be the closed subscheme $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$
1966	  %  defined by entries of the matrix formula $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$,
1967	  %  and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
1968	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
1969	  The structure morphism \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
1970	  (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is separated; equivalently, the diagonal
1971	  \(\Delta : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \times_{\mathbb{Z}}
1972	  \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a closed immersion.
1973	\end{lemma}
1974	
1975	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The intersection of the diagonal of $\grass(r,d)$ with
1976	% $U^I\times U^J$ can be seen to be the closed subscheme
1977	% $\Delta_{I,J}\subset U^I\times U^J$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
1978	% $X^J_I\,X^I - X^J =0$, and so $\grass(r,d)$ is a separated scheme."
1979	% (references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L856-L860.)
1980	\begin{proof}
1981	  \uses{def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_transition, lem:gr_diagonalRingMap_surjective, lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
1982	  \leanok
1983	  The products \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), as \(I, J\) range over the
1984	  size-\(d\) subsets, form an affine open cover of \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)
1985	  \times_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\). Separatedness may be checked on this
1986	  cover: it suffices to show that for each pair \((I,J)\) the preimage
1987	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J)\) maps by a closed immersion into
1988	  \(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\), i.e.\ that the corresponding ring
1989	  homomorphism is surjective.
1990	
1991	  By \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme} the diagonal preimage is the overlap
1992	  \(\Delta^{-1}(U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J) = U^I \cap U^J = U^I_J = \Spec
1993	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\). The restricted diagonal corresponds to the ring map
1994	  \[
1995	    \delta_{I,J} : \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \otimes_{\mathbb{Z}} \mathbb{Z}[X^J]
1996	    \longrightarrow \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J],
1997	    \qquad
1998	    X^I \otimes 1 \mapsto X^I, \quad 1 \otimes X^J \mapsto (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I,
1999	  \]
2000	  the second component being the comorphism \(\theta_{I,J}\) of
2001	  \cref{def:gr_transition} (a point of \(U^I_J\) and its image under
2002	  \(\theta_{I,J}\) are the two coordinates of a point of the diagonal). We claim
2003	  \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Its image contains all entries of \(X^I\)
2004	  (from the first factor). From the second factor it contains the entries of
2005	  \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\); taking the \(I\)-minor of this matrix, and using
2006	  \(X^I_I = 1_{d\times d}\), the image contains the entries of \((X^I_J)^{-1}\),
2007	  and in particular
2008	  \[
2009	    \delta_{I,J}(1 \otimes P^J_I) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr) = 1/P^I_J .
2010	  \]
2011	  Hence \(1/P^I_J\) lies in the image, so the image is all of
2012	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) and \(\delta_{I,J}\) is surjective. Concretely the
2013	  closed subscheme \(\Delta_{I,J} \subseteq U^I \times_{\mathbb{Z}} U^J\) is cut
2014	  out by the entries of the matrix relation
2015	  \[
2016	    X^J_I \, X^I - X^J \;=\; 0,
2017	  \]
2018	  which on the diagonal holds because \(X^J = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\) forces
2019	  \(X^J_I = (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I_I = (X^I_J)^{-1}\) and therefore \(X^J_I X^I =
2020	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I = X^J\). As each restricted diagonal is a closed immersion,
2021	  \(\Delta\) is a closed immersion and \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is separated over
2022	  \(\mathbb{Z}\).
2023	\end{proof}

2025	\section{Properness}
2026	\label{sec:gr_proper}
2027	
2028	Following Nitsure \S 1, the structure morphism \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec
2029	\mathbb{Z}\) is proper. We reduce properness to the \emph{existence} half of the
2030	valuative criterion: the Mathlib criterion \texttt{IsProper.of\_valuativeCriterion}
2031	reduces \(\mathrm{IsProper}\,\pi\) to four ingredients --- quasi-compactness,
2032	quasi-separatedness, local finiteness of type, and the valuative criterion
2033	\(\mathrm{Existence} \sqcap \mathrm{Uniqueness}\). The first three and the
2034	uniqueness half are cheap (uniqueness is free from separatedness), so the only
2035	genuine content is \(\mathrm{Existence}\): every commutative square from a
2036	valuation ring admits a diagonal filler. This existence statement is the Nitsure
2037	chart-selection argument, which we decompose into four steps E1--E4.
2038	
2039	\subsection*{Cheap ingredients and the reduction to existence}
2040	
2041	\begin{lemma}
2042	
2043	\leanok
2044	[\(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a compact space]
2045	  \label{lem:gr_compactSpace_scheme}
2046	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.compactSpace_scheme}
2047	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_affine_chart, def:gr_glued_scheme}
2048	  The underlying topological space of the glued Grassmannian scheme
2049	  \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) (\cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}) is quasi-compact: it carries the
2050	  structure of a \texttt{CompactSpace}.
2051	\end{lemma}
2052	\begin{proof}
2053	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_affine_chart}
2054	  \leanok
2055	  The glue datum (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) is indexed by the finite set
2056	  \(\{\, I \subseteq \{1,\dots,r\} : \#(I) = d \,\}\) of size-\(d\) subsets, and
2057	  each chart \(U^I = \Spec \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) (\cref{def:gr_affine_chart}) is the
2058	  \(\Spec\) of a ring, hence quasi-compact. A scheme glued from finitely many
2059	  quasi-compact charts is quasi-compact, so \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a compact
2060	  space.
2061	\end{proof}

2071	  (\cref{def:gr_to_specZ}) is quasi-compact.
2072	\end{lemma}
2073	\begin{proof}
2074	  \uses{lem:gr_compactSpace_scheme}
2075	  \leanok
2076	  The target \(\Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is affine, so quasi-compactness of \(\pi\) is
2077	  equivalent to quasi-compactness of the total space \(\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), which is
2078	  a compact space by \cref{lem:gr_compactSpace_scheme}.
2079	\end{proof}

2091	\begin{proof}

2093	  \leanok
2094	  Being locally of finite type is local on the source, so it may be checked on the
2095	  chart open cover (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}). On the chart \(U^I\) the composite
2096	  \(U^I \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \xrightarrow{\pi} \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) is, by

2098	  = \mathbb{Z}[X^I]\). The polynomial ring \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I]\) is a finitely
2099	  generated \(\mathbb{Z}\)-algebra, so this map is of finite type; hence \(\pi\) is
2100	  locally of finite type.

2120	\begin{lemma}
2121	
2122	\leanok
2123	[Uniqueness half of the valuative criterion]
2124	  \label{lem:gr_valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ}
2125	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ}
2126	  \uses{def:gr_to_specZ, lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
2127	  The structure morphism \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
2128	  (\cref{def:gr_to_specZ}) satisfies the \emph{uniqueness} part of the valuative
2129	  criterion: for a valuation ring \(R\) with fraction field \(K\), any two
2130	  morphisms \(\Spec R \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) over \(\Spec \mathbb{Z}\) that agree
2131	  on the generic point \(\Spec K\) coincide.
2132	\end{lemma}
2133	\begin{proof}
2134	  \uses{lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}
2135	  \leanok
2136	  The structure morphism is separated (\cref{lem:gr_separated_toSpecZ}), and the
2137	  uniqueness half of the valuative criterion is free for any separated morphism:
2138	  two lifts agreeing on the schematically dense generic point of \(\Spec R\)
2139	  coincide because the diagonal is a (closed, in particular separated) immersion.
2140	\end{proof}

2142	\begin{lemma}
2143	
2144	\leanok
2145	[Properness from the existence half of the valuative criterion]
2146	  \label{lem:gr_isProper_of_valuativeExistence}
2147	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.isProper_of_valuativeExistence}
2148	  \uses{lem:gr_compactSpace_scheme, lem:gr_quasiCompact_toSpecZ,
2149	    lem:gr_locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ, lem:gr_quasiSeparated_toSpecZ,
2150	    lem:gr_valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ, def:gr_to_specZ}
2151	  Suppose the structure morphism \(\pi : \mathrm{Gr}(r,d) \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\)
2152	  (\cref{def:gr_to_specZ}) satisfies the \emph{existence} part of the valuative
2153	  criterion. Then \(\pi\) is proper.
2154	\end{lemma}
2155	\begin{proof}
2156	  \uses{lem:gr_quasiCompact_toSpecZ, lem:gr_locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ,
2157	    lem:gr_quasiSeparated_toSpecZ, lem:gr_valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ}
2158	    \leanok
2159	  The Mathlib criterion \texttt{IsProper.of\_valuativeCriterion} reduces properness
2160	  of \(\pi\) to four hypotheses: \(\pi\) is quasi-compact
2161	  (\cref{lem:gr_quasiCompact_toSpecZ}), quasi-separated
2162	  (\cref{lem:gr_quasiSeparated_toSpecZ}), locally of finite type
2163	  (\cref{lem:gr_locallyOfFiniteType_toSpecZ}), and satisfies the full valuative
2164	  criterion. The full valuative criterion is the conjunction of its existence and
2165	  uniqueness halves; the uniqueness half holds by
2166	  \cref{lem:gr_valuativeUniqueness_toSpecZ}, and the existence half is the
2167	  hypothesis. Assembling the four ingredients gives properness.
2168	\end{proof}

2170	\subsection*{The existence obligation: chart selection by minimal valuation}
2171	
2172	The existence half of the valuative criterion is the genuine geometric content,
2173	and it is exactly Nitsure's chart-selection argument. A commutative square over a
2174	valuation ring presents a valuation ring \(R\) with fraction field \(K =
2175	\operatorname{Frac}(R)\), valuation \(v := \mathrm{val}_R : K \to \Gamma\) (written
2176	multiplicatively, so \(R = \{x : v(x) \le 1\}\)), a morphism \(i_1 : \Spec K \to
2177	\mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\), and a morphism \(i_2 : \Spec R \to \Spec \mathbb{Z}\) with
2178	\(i_1 \circ \pi = \Spec(R \to K) \circ i_2\); one must construct a filler
2179	\(\Spec R \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) making both triangles commute. We carry this out
2180	in four steps. The algebraic core --- the behaviour of the transition hom on a
2181	\(K'\)-minor --- is the following identity over the localised chart ring.
2182	
2183	\begin{lemma}
2184	
2185	\leanok
2186	[The minor-ratio identity]
2187	  \label{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}
2188	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}
2189	  \uses{def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det, lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet,
2190	    lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col, lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities,
2191	    lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul}
2192	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Properness" (read from
2193	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L878-L883).
2194	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2195	  % "Now consider the homomorphism
2196	  %  $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$ defined by entries of the matrix formula
2197	  %  $$g(X^J) = f((X^I_J)^{-1}\,X^I)$$
2198	  %  Then $g$ defines the same morphism $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$,
2199	  %  and moreover all $d\times d$ minors $X^J_K$ satisfy
2200	  %  $\nu(g(P^J_K)) \ge 0$."
2201	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2202	  For size-\(d\) subsets \(I, J, K\), the pre-localisation transition hom
2203	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}\) (\cref{def:gr_transition_pre}) satisfies, in \(R^I_J =
2204	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\), the minor-ratio identity
2205	  \[
2206	    \tilde\theta_{I,J}\bigl(P^J_K\bigr)\cdot P^I_J \;=\; P^I_K
2207	  \]
2208	  (where \(P^I_J, P^I_K\) are read through the structure map \(\mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to
2209	  R^I_J\)). Equivalently \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_K) = P^I_K / P^I_J\), the
2210	  determinant of the \(K\)-minor of \((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\). This generalises
2211	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_swap_mul} (the case \(K = I\), where
2212	  \(P^I_I = 1\)) and is the algebraic engine of the existence argument: pulled back
2213	  through a \(K\)-point \(f\) it gives \(g(P^J_K) = f(P^I_K)/f(P^I_J)\).
2214	\end{lemma}
2215	\begin{proof}
2216	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet, lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col,
2217	    lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}
2218	    \leanok
2219	  By \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}, \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_K) =
2220	  \det(M_K)\) is the determinant of the \(K\)-minor of the image matrix \(M =
2221	  (X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\). Splitting the \(K\)-column submatrix of the product
2222	  (\cref{lem:gr_mul_submatrix_col}) and using multiplicativity of the determinant,
2223	  \(\det(M_K) = \det\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1}\bigr)\cdot \det(X^I_K) =
2224	  \det(X^I_J)^{-1}\cdot P^I_K\). Multiplying by \(P^I_J = \det(X^I_J)\) and
2225	  cancelling \(\det(X^I_J)^{-1}\det(X^I_J) = 1\)
2226	  (\cref{lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities}) gives
2227	  \(\tilde\theta_{I,J}(P^J_K)\cdot P^I_J = P^I_K\).
2228	\end{proof}
2229	
2230	\begin{lemma}
2231	
2232	\leanok
2233	[E1 --- the \(K\)-point factors through a single chart]
2234	  \label{lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization}
2235	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_chart_factorization}
2236	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_glued_scheme, def:gr_affine_chart,
2237	    lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}
2238	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Properness" (read from
2239	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L868-L870).
2240	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2241	  % "Let $\Rr$ be a dvr, $\K$ its quotient field, and let
2242	  %  $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$ be a morphism. This is given by
2243	  %  a ring homomorphism $f : \Z[X^I] \to \K$ for some $I$."
2244	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2245	  The morphism \(i_1 : \Spec K \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) factors through a single
2246	  chart: there exist a size-\(d\) subset \(I\) and a ring homomorphism \(f : R^I =
2247	  \mathbb{Z}[X^I] \to K\) such that
2248	  \[
2249	    i_1 \;=\; \Spec(f) \circ \iota_I : \Spec K \to U^I \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d),
2250	  \]
2251	  where \(\iota_I : U^I \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is the chart immersion of the glue
2252	  datum (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}).
2253	\end{lemma}
2254	\begin{proof}
2255	  \uses{def:gr_the_glue_data, def:gr_glued_scheme, lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion}
2256	  \leanok
2257	  Since \(K\) is a field, \(\Spec K\) is a one-point space, so its image point \(x
2258	  := i_1(\ast) \in \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\) is a single point. The chart immersions
2259	  \(\{\iota_I : U^I \to \mathrm{Gr}(r,d)\}\) of the glue datum
2260	  (\cref{def:gr_the_glue_data}) are jointly surjective (their images cover the
2261	  glued scheme \cref{def:gr_glued_scheme}), so \(x\) lies in the range of some
2262	  \(\iota_I\). Each \(\iota_I\) is an open immersion
2263	  (\cref{lem:gr_chartIncl_isOpenImmersion} gives this for the chart inclusions, and
2264	  the glue datum's immersions inherit it). A morphism out of \(\Spec K\) whose
2265	  image point lies in the range of an open immersion factors uniquely through that
2266	  open immersion; hence \(i_1 = j \circ \iota_I\) for a unique \(j : \Spec K \to
2267	  U^I\). As \(U^I = \Spec R^I\) is affine, \(j = \Spec(f)\) for a unique ring map
2268	  \(f : R^I \to K\), giving the claimed factorization.
2269	\end{proof}
2270	
2271	\begin{lemma}
2272	
2273	\leanok
2274	[E2 --- minimal-valuation chart selection]
2275	  \label{lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation}
2276	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_minimal_valuation}
2277	  \uses{lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization, def:gr_minor_det,
2278	    lem:gr_minorDet_self}
2279	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Properness" (read from
2280	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L870-L876).
2281	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2282	  % "Having fixed
2283	  %  one such $I$, next choose $J$ such that $\nu(f(P^I_J))$ is minimum,
2284	  %  where $\nu : \K \to \Z\bigcup \{ \infty\}$ denotes
2285	  %  the discrete valuation. As $P^I_I = 1$, note that
2286	  %  $\nu(f(P^I_J)) \le 0$, therefore
2287	  %  $f(P^I_J) \ne 0$ in $\K$ and so the matrix $f(X^I_J)$
2288	  %  lies in $GL_d(\K)$."
2289	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). With `v := ValuationRing.valuation
2290	  %   R K`, choose `J : {J : Finset (Fin r) // J.card = d}` maximizing
2291	  %   `v (f (minorDet d r I J))` over the finite index set, and conclude
2292	  %   `f (minorDet d r I J) ≠ 0` (equivalently `v (f (minorDet d r I J)) ≥ 1`).
2293	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2294	  Fix \(I\) and \(f : R^I \to K\) as in \cref{lem:gr_existence_chart_factorization},
2295	  and let \(v := \mathrm{val}_R : K \to \Gamma\) be the (multiplicative) valuation
2296	  of \(R\). Over the finite index set \(\{\, J : \#(J) = d \,\}\) choose \(J\)
2297	  \emph{maximising} \(v\bigl(f(P^I_J)\bigr)\) (Nitsure's minimal additive valuation
2298	  \(\nu\) is the maximal multiplicative \(v\)). Then \(I\) itself is a candidate and
2299	  \(P^I_I = 1\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_self}) gives \(v(f(P^I_I)) = v(1) = 1\), so
2300	  the maximum satisfies \(v(f(P^I_J)) \ge 1 > 0\). In particular \(f(P^I_J) \ne 0\),
2301	  so the matrix \(f(X^I_J)\) is invertible over \(K\).
2302	\end{lemma}
2303	\begin{proof}
2304	  \uses{lem:gr_minorDet_self, def:gr_minor_det}
2305	  \leanok
2306	  The index set of size-\(d\) subsets is finite and nonempty (\(I\) is a member),
2307	  so the function \(J \mapsto v(f(P^I_J))\) attains a maximum at some \(J\). Since
2308	  \(P^I_I = 1\) (\cref{lem:gr_minorDet_self}) and \(v(1) = 1\), the value at \(I\)
2309	  is \(1\); hence the maximum is \(\ge 1\), so \(v(f(P^I_J)) \ne 0\) in \(\Gamma\),
2310	  i.e.\ \(f(P^I_J) \ne 0\). A square matrix over the field \(K\) with nonzero
2311	  determinant \(f(P^I_J) = \det f(X^I_J)\) is invertible.
2312	\end{proof}
2313	
2314	\begin{lemma}
2315	
2316	\leanok
2317	[E3 --- entries land in \(R\); \(g\) factors through the valuation ring]
2318	  \label{lem:gr_existence_factor_through_valuation_ring}
2319	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_factor_through_valuationRing}
2320	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul, lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation,
2321	    lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet, lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross,
2322	    lem:mathlib_away_lift, def:gr_transition_pre, def:gr_minor_det}
2323	  % SOURCE: [Nitsure], \S 1, sub-section "Properness" (read from
2324	  % references/nitsure-hilbert-quot-src/nitsure-hilbert-quot.tex, L881-L887).
2325	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
2326	  % "Then $g$ defines the same morphism $\varphi : \spec \K \to \grass(r,d)$,
2327	  %  and moreover all $d\times d$ minors $X^J_K$ satisfy
2328	  %  $\nu(g(P^J_K)) \ge 0$.
2329	  %  As the minor $X^J_J$ is identity, it follows from the above that in fact
2330	  %  $\nu(g(x^J_{p,q}))\ge 0$ for all entries of $X^J$.
2331	  %  Therefore, the map $g : \Z[X^J] \to \K$
2332	  %  factors uniquely via $\Rr \subset \K$."
2333	  % LEAN SIGNATURE (intended scaffold target). Set
2334	  %   `g := f' ∘ transitionPreMap d r I J`, where `f' : R^I_J → K` is the
2335	  %   `IsLocalization.Away.lift` of `f` along `minorDet d r I J` (a unit by E2).
2336	  %   Prove `∀ x, g x ∈ (algebraMap R K).range`, hence factor `g = (algebraMap R K)
2337	  %   ∘ g'` for a unique ring hom `g' : R^J → R`.
2338	  \textit{Source: [Nitsure], \S 1.}
2339	  Define \(g : R^J = \mathbb{Z}[X^J] \to K\) by the matrix formula \(g(X^J) =
2340	  f\bigl((X^I_J)^{-1} X^I\bigr)\); concretely \(g = f' \circ \tilde\theta_{I,J}\),
2341	  where \(f' : R^I_J \to K\) is the extension of \(f\) to the away-localisation
2342	  \(R^I_J = \mathbb{Z}[X^I, 1/P^I_J]\) (well-defined by the universal property
2343	  \cref{lem:mathlib_away_lift}, since \(f(P^I_J)\) is a unit of \(K\) by
2344	  \cref{lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation}). Then every value \(g(P^J_K)\) lies in
2345	  \(R\), and consequently \(g\) factors uniquely as \(g = (R \hookrightarrow K)
2346	  \circ g'\) for a ring homomorphism \(g' : R^J \to R\). Moreover \(g\) defines the
2347	  same \(K\)-point as \(f\).
2348	\end{lemma}
2349	\begin{proof}
2350	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul, lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation,
2351	    lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet, lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross}
2352	    \leanok
2353	  Applying \(f'\) to the minor-ratio identity
2354	  (\cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}), and using that \(f'\) extends
2355	  \(f\), gives for every size-\(d\) subset \(K'\)
2356	  \[
2357	    g\bigl(P^J_{K'}\bigr)\cdot f(P^I_J) \;=\; f(P^I_{K'}),
2358	    \qquad\text{so}\qquad
2359	    g\bigl(P^J_{K'}\bigr) \;=\; \frac{f(P^I_{K'})}{f(P^I_J)} .
2360	  \]
2361	  By the maximality in \cref{lem:gr_existence_minimal_valuation},
2362	  \(v(f(P^I_{K'})) \le v(f(P^I_J))\), hence \(v\bigl(g(P^J_{K'})\bigr) =
2363	  v(f(P^I_{K'}))/v(f(P^I_J)) \le 1\), i.e.\ \(g(P^J_{K'}) \in R\). (That
2364	  \(f(P^I_J)\) is a unit and the cross minors map to units is recorded by
2365	  \cref{lem:gr_isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross}, via the determinant identity of
2366	  \cref{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet}.) Now the \(J\)-minor \(X^J_J\) of \(X^J\)
2367	  is the identity, so cofactor expansion expresses each free entry \(x^J_{p,q}\)
2368	  (\(q \notin J\)) as, up to sign, the minor \(P^J_{K'}\) with \(K' = (J \setminus
2369	  \{j_p\}) \cup \{q\}\) --- the determinant obtained by replacing the \(p\)-th
2370	  identity column of \(X^J_J\) by column \(q\). Hence \(g(x^J_{p,q}) = \pm
2371	  g(P^J_{K'}) \in R\), while the entries with \(q \in J\) are \(0\) or \(1\),
2372	  already in \(R\). (This entry-as-minor sign bookkeeping is the one sub-step with
2373	  no pre-existing matrix-algebra scaffold; it requires expanding the determinant of
2374	  a column-substituted identity matrix.) Since \(g\) sends every generator of
2375	  \(\mathbb{Z}[X^J]\) into \(R\), it factors uniquely through the subring \(R
2376	  \subseteq K\) as \(g = (R \hookrightarrow K) \circ g'\) with \(g' : R^J \to R\).
2377	\end{proof}

2379	\begin{lemma}
2380	
2381	\leanok
2382	[E3 ratio core --- the localised minor-ratio identity (coverage)]
2383	  \label{lem:gr_existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}
2384	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.existence_lift_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul}
2385	  \uses{lem:gr_transitionPreMap_minorDet_mul, lem:mathlib_away_lift}
